exercisesoccams-razor5 atoms2✓ · 2✗
probmods2-occams-razor/ex1.2exercisesdistribution8 runsTV=0.00TV=0
prompt
Number-game model. The hypothesis space contains rule-based concepts (multiples_of_N, powers_of_N for N=1..11; evens; odds) over the integers in [1, 20]. Implement similarity-based interval hypotheses (integers in [a, b]) so that: - genSetFromInterval(a, b) returns all integers from a through b inclusive, - makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(start, end) returns names 'interval_a_b' for every (a, b) with start <= a < b <= end, - getSetFromHypothesis(rule) dispatches on the rule's prefix to produce the set of integers it denotes (handling multiples_, powers_, evens, odds, and interval_). These helpers are given (include them or equivalent in your program): 
var maxNumber = 20;
var filterByInRange = function(set) {
  var inRange = function(v) { v <= maxNumber && v >= 0 };
  return _.uniq(filter(inRange, set));
};
var genEvens = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return v % 2 == 0 }, _.range(1, maxNumber));
};
var genOdds = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return (v + 1) % 2 == 0 }, _.range(1, maxNumber));
};
var genMultiples = function(base) {
  var multiples = map(function(v) { return base * v }, _.range(maxNumber));
  return filterByInRange(multiples);
};
var genPowers = function(base) {
  var powers = map(function(v) { return Math.pow(base, v) }, _.range(maxNumber));
  return filterByInRange(powers);
};
var inSet = function(val, set) { return _.includes(set, val); };
var makeRuleHypothesisSpace = function() {
  var multipleRules = map(function(b) { return 'multiples_of_' + b }, _.range(1, 12));
  var powerRules    = map(function(b) { return 'powers_of_' + b },    _.range(1, 12));
  return multipleRules.concat(powerRules).concat(['evens', 'odds']);
};
Build learnConcept(examples, testQuery) that mixes rule and interval hypotheses 50/50 in the prior, draws a hypothesis, observes each example via Categorical({vs: set}), and returns {hypothesis, testQueryResponse: inSet(testQuery, set)}. End your program with learnConcept([3, 10], 12) returning the joint posterior.
groundtruth code
var maxNumber = 20;
var filterByInRange =  function(set) {
  var inRange = function(v) {v <= maxNumber && v >= 0};
  return _.uniq(filter(inRange, set));
};
var genEvens = function() {
  return filter(function(v) {return v % 2 == 0}, _.range(1, maxNumber));
};
var genOdds = function() {
  return filter(function(v) {return (v + 1) % 2 == 0}, _.range(1, maxNumber));
};
var genMultiples = function(base) {
  var multiples = map(function(v) {return base * v}, _.range(maxNumber));
  return filterByInRange(multiples);
};
var genPowers = function(base) {
  var powers = map(function(v) {return Math.pow(base, v)}, _.range(maxNumber));
  return filterByInRange(powers);
};
var inSet = function(val, set) { return _.includes(set, val); };
var makeRuleHypothesisSpace = function() {
  var multipleRules = map(function(base) {return 'multiples_of_' + base}, _.range(1, 12));
  var powerRules = map(function(base) {return 'powers_of_' + base}, _.range(1, 12));
  return multipleRules.concat(powerRules).concat(['evens', 'odds']);
};
var genSetFromInterval = function(a, b) { return _.range(a, b+1); };

var makeIntervalHypothesisSpace = function(start, end) {
  var allIntervals = _.flatten(map(function(s) {
    return map(function(e) { [s, e] }, genSetFromInterval(s+1, end));
  }, genSetFromInterval(start, end)));
  return map(function(x) { 'interval_' + x[0] + '_' + x[1] }, allIntervals);
};

var getSetFromHypothesis = function(rule) {
  var parts = rule.split('_');
  return (parts[0] == 'multiples' ? genMultiples(_.parseInt(parts[2])) :
          parts[0] == 'powers' ? genPowers(_.parseInt(parts[2])) :
          parts[0] == 'evens' ? genEvens() :
          parts[0] == 'odds' ? genOdds() :
          parts[0] == 'interval' ? genSetFromInterval(_.parseInt(parts[1]), _.parseInt(parts[2])) :
          console.error('unknown rule' + rule));
};

var learnConcept = function(examples, testQuery) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var rules = makeRuleHypothesisSpace();
    var intervals = makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(1, maxNumber);
    var hypothesis = flip(0.5) ? uniformDraw(rules) : uniformDraw(intervals);
    var set = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);
    mapData({data: examples}, function(example) {
      observe(Categorical({vs: set}), example);
    });
    return {hypothesis: hypothesis,
            testQueryResponse: inSet(testQuery, set)};
  });
};
var ANSWER = (learnConcept([3, 10], 12));
generated code
haiku-45-noprimer-v3 failhaiku-45-primer-v3 failhaiku-45-think-noprimer-v3 failhaiku-45-think-primer-v3 failsonnet-46-noprimer-v3 failsonnet-46-primer-v3 TV=0sonnet-46-think-noprimer-v3 failsonnet-46-think-primer-v3 TV=0
Error: cpsInnerStatement
var maxNumber = 20;

var filterByInRange = function(set) {
  var inRange = function(v) { return v <= maxNumber && v >= 1; };
  return _.uniq(filter(inRange, set));
};

var genEvens = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return v % 2 == 0 }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};

var genOdds = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return v % 2 == 1 }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};

var genMultiples = function(base) {
  var multiples = map(function(v) { return base * v }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(multiples);
};

var genPowers = function(base) {
  var powers = map(function(v) { return Math.pow(base, v) }, _.range(0, maxNumber));
  return filterByInRange(powers);
};

var inSet = function(val, set) { 
  return _.includes(set, val); 
};

var makeRuleHypothesisSpace = function() {
  var multipleRules = map(function(b) { return 'multiples_of_' + b }, _.range(1, 12));
  var powerRules = map(function(b) { return 'powers_of_' + b }, _.range(1, 12));
  return multipleRules.concat(powerRules).concat(['evens', 'odds']);
};

var genSetFromInterval = function(a, b) {
  return _.range(a, b + 1);
};

var makeIntervalHypothesisSpace = function(start, end) {
  var intervals = [];
  for (var a = start; a < end; a++) {
    for (var b = a + 1; b <= end; b++) {
      intervals.push('interval_' + a + '_' + b);
    }
  }
  return intervals;
};

var getSetFromHypothesis = function(rule) {
  if (rule.indexOf('multiples_of_') === 0) {
    var base = parseInt(rule.split('_').pop());
    return genMultiples(base);
  } else if (rule.indexOf('powers_of_') === 0) {
    var base = parseInt(rule.split('_').pop());
    return genPowers(base);
  } else if (rule === 'evens') {
    return genEvens();
  } else if (rule === 'odds') {
    return genOdds();
  } else if (rule.indexOf('interval_') === 0) {
    var parts = rule.split('_');
    var a = parseInt(parts[1]);
    var b = parseInt(parts[2]);
    return genSetFromInterval(a, b);
  }
  return [];
};

var learnConcept = function(examples, testQuery) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var ruleHypotheses = makeRuleHypothesisSpace();
    var intervalHypotheses = makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(1, maxNumber);
    var allHypotheses = ruleHypotheses.concat(intervalHypotheses);
    
    var hypothesis = categorical({
      vs: allHypotheses,
      ps: map(function(h) {
        return 0.5 / ruleHypotheses.length + 0.5 / intervalHypotheses.length;
      }, allHypotheses)
    });
    
    var set = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);
    
    mapData({data: examples}, function(example) {
      observe(Categorical({vs: set, ps: map(function() { return 1; }, set)}), example);
    });
    
    var testQueryResponse = inSet(testQuery, set);
    
    return {hypothesis: hypothesis, testQueryResponse: testQueryResponse};
  });
};

var ANSWER = learnConcept([3, 10], 12);
Error: cpsInnerStatement
var maxNumber = 20;

var filterByInRange = function(set) {
  var inRange = function(v) { return v <= maxNumber && v >= 1; };
  return _.uniq(filter(inRange, set));
};

var genEvens = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return v % 2 == 0; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};

var genOdds = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return v % 2 == 1; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};

var genMultiples = function(base) {
  var multiples = map(function(v) { return base * v; }, _.range(0, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(multiples);
};

var genPowers = function(base) {
  var powers = map(function(v) { return Math.pow(base, v); }, _.range(0, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(powers);
};

var inSet = function(val, set) { return _.includes(set, val); };

var makeRuleHypothesisSpace = function() {
  var multipleRules = map(function(b) { return 'multiples_of_' + b; }, _.range(1, 12));
  var powerRules = map(function(b) { return 'powers_of_' + b; }, _.range(1, 12));
  return multipleRules.concat(powerRules).concat(['evens', 'odds']);
};

var genSetFromInterval = function(a, b) {
  return filter(function(v) { return v >= a && v <= b; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};

var makeIntervalHypothesisSpace = function(start, end) {
  var intervals = [];
  var i = start;
  while (i < end) {
    var j = i + 1;
    while (j <= end) {
      intervals.push('interval_' + i + '_' + j);
      j = j + 1;
    }
    i = i + 1;
  }
  return intervals;
};

var getSetFromHypothesis = function(rule) {
  if (rule === 'evens') {
    return genEvens();
  } else if (rule === 'odds') {
    return genOdds();
  } else if (rule.substring(0, 10) === 'multiples_') {
    var base = _.parseInt(rule.substring(10));
    return genMultiples(base);
  } else if (rule.substring(0, 8) === 'powers_of_') {
    var base = _.parseInt(rule.substring(10));
    return genPowers(base);
  } else if (rule.substring(0, 9) === 'interval_') {
    var parts = rule.substring(9).split('_');
    var a = _.parseInt(parts[0]);
    var b = _.parseInt(parts[1]);
    return genSetFromInterval(a, b);
  } else {
    return [];
  }
};

var learnConcept = function(examples, testQuery) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var ruleHypotheses = makeRuleHypothesisSpace();
    var intervalHypotheses = makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(1, maxNumber);
    var allHypotheses = ruleHypotheses.concat(intervalHypotheses);
    
    var hypothesisType = flip(0.5) ? 'rule' : 'interval';
    var hypothesis = hypothesisType === 'rule' 
      ? uniformDraw(ruleHypotheses)
      : uniformDraw(intervalHypotheses);
    
    var set = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);
    
    mapData({data: examples}, function(example) {
      observe(Categorical({vs: set, ps: map(function() { return 1.0 / set.length; }, set)}), example);
    });
    
    var testQueryResponse = inSet(testQuery, set);
    
    return {hypothesis: hypothesis, testQueryResponse: testQueryResponse};
  });
};

var ANSWER = learnConcept([3, 10], 12);
Error: cpsInnerStatement
var maxNumber = 20;

var filterByInRange = function(set) {
  var inRange = function(v) { return v <= maxNumber && v >= 0; };
  return _.uniq(filter(inRange, set));
};

var genEvens = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return v % 2 == 0; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};

var genOdds = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return (v + 1) % 2 == 0; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};

var genMultiples = function(base) {
  var multiples = map(function(v) { return base * v; }, _.range(0, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(multiples);
};

var genPowers = function(base) {
  var powers = map(function(v) { return Math.pow(base, v); }, _.range(0, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(powers);
};

var inSet = function(val, set) { return _.includes(set, val); };

var makeRuleHypothesisSpace = function() {
  var multipleRules = map(function(b) { return 'multiples_of_' + b; }, _.range(1, 12));
  var powerRules = map(function(b) { return 'powers_of_' + b; }, _.range(1, 12));
  return multipleRules.concat(powerRules).concat(['evens', 'odds']);
};

var genSetFromInterval = function(a, b) {
  return _.range(a, b + 1);
};

var makeIntervalHypothesisSpace = function(start, end) {
  var intervals = [];
  for (var a = start; a < end; a++) {
    for (var b = a + 1; b <= end; b++) {
      intervals.push('interval_' + a + '_' + b);
    }
  }
  return intervals;
};

var getSetFromHypothesis = function(rule) {
  if (_.startsWith(rule, 'multiples_of_')) {
    var base = parseInt(rule.split('_')[2]);
    return genMultiples(base);
  } else if (_.startsWith(rule, 'powers_of_')) {
    var base = parseInt(rule.split('_')[2]);
    return genPowers(base);
  } else if (rule === 'evens') {
    return genEvens();
  } else if (rule === 'odds') {
    return genOdds();
  } else if (_.startsWith(rule, 'interval_')) {
    var parts = rule.split('_');
    var a = parseInt(parts[1]);
    var b = parseInt(parts[2]);
    return genSetFromInterval(a, b);
  }
  return [];
};

var learnConcept = function(examples, testQuery) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var ruleHypotheses = makeRuleHypothesisSpace();
    var intervalHypotheses = makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(1, maxNumber);
    
    // 50/50 prior over rule vs. interval hypotheses
    var hypothesisType = uniformDraw(['rule', 'interval']);
    var hypothesis = hypothesisType === 'rule' ? 
      uniformDraw(ruleHypotheses) : 
      uniformDraw(intervalHypotheses);
    
    var set = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);
    
    // Observe each example
    forEach(examples, function(example) {
      observe(Categorical({vs: set}), example);
    });
    
    return {
      hypothesis: hypothesis,
      testQueryResponse: inSet(testQuery, set)
    };
  });
};

var ANSWER = learnConcept([3, 10], 12);
Error: cpsInnerStatement
var maxNumber = 20;

var filterByInRange = function(set) {
  var inRange = function(v) { return v <= maxNumber && v >= 1; };
  return _.uniq(filter(inRange, set));
};

var genEvens = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return v % 2 == 0; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};

var genOdds = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return (v + 1) % 2 == 0; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};

var genMultiples = function(base) {
  var multiples = map(function(v) { return base * v; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(multiples);
};

var genPowers = function(base) {
  var powers = map(function(v) { return Math.pow(base, v); }, _.range(0, 20));
  return filterByInRange(powers);
};

var inSet = function(val, set) { return _.includes(set, val); };

var makeRuleHypothesisSpace = function() {
  var multipleRules = map(function(b) { return 'multiples_of_' + b; }, _.range(1, 12));
  var powerRules    = map(function(b) { return 'powers_of_' + b; }, _.range(1, 12));
  return multipleRules.concat(powerRules).concat(['evens', 'odds']);
};

var genSetFromInterval = function(a, b) {
  return _.range(a, b + 1);
};

var makeIntervalHypothesisSpace = function(start, end) {
  var intervals = [];
  var i = start;
  while (i < end) {
    var j = i + 1;
    while (j <= end) {
      intervals.push('interval_' + i + '_' + j);
      j = j + 1;
    }
    i = i + 1;
  }
  return intervals;
};

var getSetFromHypothesis = function(rule) {
  if (rule === 'evens') {
    return genEvens();
  } else if (rule === 'odds') {
    return genOdds();
  } else if (_.includes(rule, 'multiples_of_')) {
    var parts = rule.split('_');
    var base = parseInt(parts[2]);
    return genMultiples(base);
  } else if (_.includes(rule, 'powers_of_')) {
    var parts = rule.split('_');
    var base = parseInt(parts[2]);
    return genPowers(base);
  } else if (_.includes(rule, 'interval_')) {
    var parts = rule.split('_');
    var a = parseInt(parts[1]);
    var b = parseInt(parts[2]);
    return genSetFromInterval(a, b);
  } else {
    return [];
  }
};

var learnConcept = function(examples, testQuery) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var ruleHyps = makeRuleHypothesisSpace();
    var intervalHyps = makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(1, maxNumber);
    
    var isRule = flip(0.5);
    var hypothesis = isRule ? 
      uniformDraw(ruleHyps) : 
      uniformDraw(intervalHyps);
    
    var set = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);
    
    mapData({data: examples}, function(example) {
      observe(Categorical({vs: set}), example);
    });
    
    return {
      hypothesis: hypothesis,
      testQueryResponse: inSet(testQuery, set)
    };
  });
};

var ANSWER = learnConcept([3, 10], 12);
program exited 0 but produced no output (likely silent failure or undefined ANSWER)
var maxNumber = 20;
var filterByInRange = function(set) {
  var inRange = function(v) { return v <= maxNumber && v >= 1; };
  return _.uniq(filter(inRange, set));
};
var genEvens = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return v % 2 == 0; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};
var genOdds = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return (v + 1) % 2 == 0; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};
var genMultiples = function(base) {
  var multiples = map(function(v) { return base * v; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(multiples);
};
var genPowers = function(base) {
  var powers = map(function(v) { return Math.pow(base, v); }, _.range(0, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(powers);
};
var inSet = function(val, set) { return _.includes(set, val); };

var makeRuleHypothesisSpace = function() {
  var multipleRules = map(function(b) { return 'multiples_of_' + b; }, _.range(1, 12));
  var powerRules    = map(function(b) { return 'powers_of_' + b; },    _.range(1, 12));
  return multipleRules.concat(powerRules).concat(['evens', 'odds']);
};

var genSetFromInterval = function(a, b) {
  return _.range(a, b + 1);
};

var makeIntervalHypothesisSpace = function(start, end) {
  var names = [];
  var as = _.range(start, end);
  return _.flatten(map(function(a) {
    var bs = _.range(a + 1, end + 1);
    return map(function(b) {
      return 'interval_' + a + '_' + b;
    }, bs);
  }, as));
};

var getSetFromHypothesis = function(rule) {
  if (rule.indexOf('multiples_of_') === 0) {
    var base = parseInt(rule.slice('multiples_of_'.length));
    return genMultiples(base);
  } else if (rule.indexOf('powers_of_') === 0) {
    var base = parseInt(rule.slice('powers_of_'.length));
    return genPowers(base);
  } else if (rule === 'evens') {
    return genEvens();
  } else if (rule === 'odds') {
    return genOdds();
  } else if (rule.indexOf('interval_') === 0) {
    var parts = rule.slice('interval_'.length).split('_');
    var a = parseInt(parts[0]);
    var b = parseInt(parts[1]);
    return genSetFromInterval(a, b);
  }
};

var learnConcept = function(examples, testQuery) {
  var ruleHypotheses = makeRuleHypothesisSpace();
  var intervalHypotheses = makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(1, maxNumber);
  
  var numRule = ruleHypotheses.length;
  var numInterval = intervalHypotheses.length;
  
  // 50/50 split between rule and interval hypotheses
  // Each rule hypothesis gets weight: 0.5 / numRule
  // Each interval hypothesis gets weight: 0.5 / numInterval
  
  var allHypotheses = ruleHypotheses.concat(intervalHypotheses);
  var ruleWeight = 0.5 / numRule;
  var intervalWeight = 0.5 / numInterval;
  var weights = map(function(h) {
    return _.includes(ruleHypotheses, h) ? ruleWeight : intervalWeight;
  }, allHypotheses);
  
  var model = function() {
    var hypothesis = categorical({ vs: allHypotheses, ps: weights });
    var set = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);
    
    // Observe each example: it must be in the set
    // Using factor with log probability of observing from Categorical
    var setSize = set.length;
    
    // Each example is observed as drawn from Categorical over the set
    mapData({ data: examples }, function(example) {
      var exampleInSet = inSet(example, set);
      factor(exampleInSet ? -Math.log(setSize) : -Infinity);
    });
    
    var testQueryResponse = inSet(testQuery, set);
    return { hypothesis: hypothesis, testQueryResponse: testQueryResponse };
  };
  
  return Infer({ method: 'enumerate' }, model);
};

var ANSWER = learnConcept([3, 10], 12);
var maxNumber = 20;

var filterByInRange = function(set) {
  var inRange = function(v) { return v <= maxNumber && v >= 1; };
  return _.uniq(filter(inRange, set));
};

var genEvens = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return v % 2 == 0; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};

var genOdds = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return (v + 1) % 2 == 0; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};

var genMultiples = function(base) {
  var multiples = map(function(v) { return base * v; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(multiples);
};

var genPowers = function(base) {
  var powers = map(function(v) { return Math.pow(base, v); }, _.range(0, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(powers);
};

var inSet = function(val, set) { return _.includes(set, val); };

var makeRuleHypothesisSpace = function() {
  var multipleRules = map(function(b) { return 'multiples_of_' + b; }, _.range(1, 12));
  var powerRules    = map(function(b) { return 'powers_of_' + b; },    _.range(1, 12));
  return multipleRules.concat(powerRules).concat(['evens', 'odds']);
};

var genSetFromInterval = function(a, b) {
  return _.range(a, b + 1);
};

var makeIntervalHypothesisSpace = function(start, end) {
  var pairs = [];
  var as = _.range(start, end);
  return _.flatten(map(function(a) {
    var bs = _.range(a + 1, end + 1);
    return map(function(b) {
      return 'interval_' + a + '_' + b;
    }, bs);
  }, as));
};

var getSetFromHypothesis = function(rule) {
  var isMultiples = rule.indexOf('multiples_of_') === 0;
  var isPowers    = rule.indexOf('powers_of_') === 0;
  var isInterval  = rule.indexOf('interval_') === 0;
  return isMultiples ? (function() {
    var base = _.parseInt(rule.slice('multiples_of_'.length));
    return genMultiples(base);
  })() :
  isPowers ? (function() {
    var base = _.parseInt(rule.slice('powers_of_'.length));
    return genPowers(base);
  })() :
  isInterval ? (function() {
    var rest = rule.slice('interval_'.length);
    // rest is like "a_b"
    // find the underscore separating a and b
    // a and b could be multi-digit, split on first underscore
    var firstUnderscore = rest.indexOf('_');
    var a = _.parseInt(rest.slice(0, firstUnderscore));
    var b = _.parseInt(rest.slice(firstUnderscore + 1));
    return genSetFromInterval(a, b);
  })() :
  rule === 'evens' ? genEvens() :
  rule === 'odds'  ? genOdds()  :
  [];
};

var ruleHypotheses     = makeRuleHypothesisSpace();
var intervalHypotheses = makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(1, maxNumber);

var numRules     = ruleHypotheses.length;
var numIntervals = intervalHypotheses.length;

var learnConcept = function(examples, testQuery) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    // 50/50 prior between rule-based and interval hypotheses
    var useRule = flip(0.5);
    var hypothesis = useRule
      ? ruleHypotheses[randomInteger(numRules)]
      : intervalHypotheses[randomInteger(numIntervals)];

    var set = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);

    // Each example is observed as drawn uniformly from the set
    var setSize = set.length;
    condition(setSize > 0);

    // Observe each example
    mapData({data: examples}, function(ex) {
      observe(Categorical({vs: set}), ex);
    });

    var testQueryResponse = inSet(testQuery, set);

    return {hypothesis: hypothesis, testQueryResponse: testQueryResponse};
  });
};

var ANSWER = learnConcept([3, 10], 12);
program exited 0 but produced no output (likely silent failure or undefined ANSWER)
var maxNumber = 20;

var filterByInRange = function(set) {
  var inRange = function(v) { return v <= maxNumber && v >= 1; };
  return _.uniq(filter(inRange, set));
};

var genEvens = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return v % 2 === 0; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};

var genOdds = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return (v + 1) % 2 === 0; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};

var genMultiples = function(base) {
  var multiples = map(function(v) { return base * v; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(multiples);
};

var genPowers = function(base) {
  var powers = map(function(v) { return Math.pow(base, v); }, _.range(0, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(powers);
};

var inSet = function(val, set) { return _.includes(set, val); };

var makeRuleHypothesisSpace = function() {
  var multipleRules = map(function(b) { return 'multiples_of_' + b; }, _.range(1, 12));
  var powerRules    = map(function(b) { return 'powers_of_' + b; },    _.range(1, 12));
  return multipleRules.concat(powerRules).concat(['evens', 'odds']);
};

// Returns all integers from a through b inclusive
var genSetFromInterval = function(a, b) {
  return _.range(a, b + 1);
};

// Returns 'interval_a_b' names for every (a,b) with start <= a < b <= end
var makeIntervalHypothesisSpace = function(start, end) {
  var as = _.range(start, end);          // a from start to end-1
  return _.flatten(map(function(a) {
    var bs = _.range(a + 1, end + 1);    // b from a+1 to end
    return map(function(b) {
      return 'interval_' + a + '_' + b;
    }, bs);
  }, as));
};

// Dispatch on rule prefix to produce the set of integers it denotes
var getSetFromHypothesis = function(rule) {
  if (rule === 'evens') {
    return genEvens();
  } else if (rule === 'odds') {
    return genOdds();
  } else if (rule.indexOf('multiples_of_') === 0) {
    var base = parseInt(rule.slice('multiples_of_'.length));
    return genMultiples(base);
  } else if (rule.indexOf('powers_of_') === 0) {
    var base2 = parseInt(rule.slice('powers_of_'.length));
    return genPowers(base2);
  } else if (rule.indexOf('interval_') === 0) {
    var suffix = rule.slice('interval_'.length);
    var underscoreIdx = suffix.indexOf('_');
    var a = parseInt(suffix.slice(0, underscoreIdx));
    var b = parseInt(suffix.slice(underscoreIdx + 1));
    return genSetFromInterval(a, b);
  }
};

var learnConcept = function(examples, testQuery) {
  var ruleHypotheses     = makeRuleHypothesisSpace();
  var intervalHypotheses = makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(1, maxNumber);

  return Infer({ method: 'enumerate' }, function() {
    // 50/50 prior: rule-based vs interval-based
    var useRule      = flip(0.5);
    var hypotheses   = useRule ? ruleHypotheses : intervalHypotheses;

    // Uniform draw within the chosen family
    var hypothesis   = uniformDraw(hypotheses);
    var set          = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);

    // Observe each example as a draw from Categorical over the set
    mapData({ data: examples }, function(ex) {
      observe(Categorical({ vs: set }), ex);
    });

    var testQueryResponse = inSet(testQuery, set);

    return { hypothesis: hypothesis, testQueryResponse: testQueryResponse };
  });
};

var ANSWER = learnConcept([3, 10], 12);
var maxNumber = 20;

var filterByInRange = function(set) {
  var inRange = function(v) { return v <= maxNumber && v >= 1; };
  return _.uniq(filter(inRange, set));
};

var genEvens = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return v % 2 === 0; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};

var genOdds = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return (v + 1) % 2 === 0; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};

var genMultiples = function(base) {
  var multiples = map(function(v) { return base * v; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(multiples);
};

var genPowers = function(base) {
  var powers = map(function(v) { return Math.pow(base, v); }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(powers);
};

var inSet = function(val, set) { return _.includes(set, val); };

var makeRuleHypothesisSpace = function() {
  var multipleRules = map(function(b) { return 'multiples_of_' + b; }, _.range(1, 12));
  var powerRules    = map(function(b) { return 'powers_of_' + b;    }, _.range(1, 12));
  return multipleRules.concat(powerRules).concat(['evens', 'odds']);
};

var genSetFromInterval = function(a, b) {
  return _.range(a, b + 1);
};

var makeIntervalHypothesisSpace = function(start, end) {
  var as = _.range(start, end);
  return _.flatten(map(function(a) {
    var bs = _.range(a + 1, end + 1);
    return map(function(b) {
      return 'interval_' + a + '_' + b;
    }, bs);
  }, as));
};

var getSetFromHypothesis = function(rule) {
  if (rule === 'evens') {
    return genEvens();
  } else if (rule === 'odds') {
    return genOdds();
  } else if (rule.indexOf('multiples_of_') === 0) {
    var mBase = _.parseInt(rule.slice('multiples_of_'.length));
    return genMultiples(mBase);
  } else if (rule.indexOf('powers_of_') === 0) {
    var pBase = _.parseInt(rule.slice('powers_of_'.length));
    return genPowers(pBase);
  } else {
    // interval_a_b
    var body  = rule.slice('interval_'.length);
    var parts = body.split('_');
    var a = _.parseInt(parts[0]);
    var b = _.parseInt(parts[1]);
    return genSetFromInterval(a, b);
  }
};

var learnConcept = function(examples, testQuery) {
  var ruleSpace     = makeRuleHypothesisSpace();
  var intervalSpace = makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(1, maxNumber);

  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    // 50/50 prior between rule-based and interval hypotheses
    var useRule    = flip(0.5);
    var space      = useRule ? ruleSpace : intervalSpace;
    var hypothesis = space[randomInteger(space.length)];
    var set        = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);

    // Each example is observed as drawn uniformly from the concept's extension
    mapData({data: examples}, function(example) {
      observe(Categorical({vs: set}), example);
    });

    var testQueryResponse = inSet(testQuery, set);
    return {hypothesis: hypothesis, testQueryResponse: testQueryResponse};
  });
};

var ANSWER = learnConcept([3, 10], 12);

probmods2-occams-razor/ex1.3exercisesvalue8 runs✗val-
prompt
Using the number-game model with rule + interval hypotheses (50/50 mix of rule-based and interval_a_b hypotheses) over integers [1, 20]: compute for each query in [1, 20] the expected probability that the query is in the inferred concept, given examples = [3, 6, 9]. Helpers (include them in your program): 
var maxNumber = 20;
var filterByInRange = function(set) {
  var inRange = function(v) { v <= maxNumber && v >= 0 };
  return _.uniq(filter(inRange, set));
};
var genEvens = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return v % 2 == 0 }, _.range(1, maxNumber));
};
var genOdds = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return (v + 1) % 2 == 0 }, _.range(1, maxNumber));
};
var genMultiples = function(base) {
  var multiples = map(function(v) { return base * v }, _.range(maxNumber));
  return filterByInRange(multiples);
};
var genPowers = function(base) {
  var powers = map(function(v) { return Math.pow(base, v) }, _.range(maxNumber));
  return filterByInRange(powers);
};
var inSet = function(val, set) { return _.includes(set, val); };
var makeRuleHypothesisSpace = function() {
  var multipleRules = map(function(b) { return 'multiples_of_' + b }, _.range(1, 12));
  var powerRules    = map(function(b) { return 'powers_of_' + b },    _.range(1, 12));
  return multipleRules.concat(powerRules).concat(['evens', 'odds']);
};
var genSetFromInterval = function(a, b) { return _.range(a, b+1); };
var makeIntervalHypothesisSpace = function(start, end) {
  var allIntervals = _.flatten(map(function(s) {
    return map(function(e) { [s, e] }, genSetFromInterval(s+1, end));
  }, genSetFromInterval(start, end)));
  return map(function(x) { 'interval_' + x[0] + '_' + x[1] }, allIntervals);
};
var getSetFromHypothesis = function(rule) {
  var parts = rule.split('_');
  return parts[0] == 'multiples' ? genMultiples(_.parseInt(parts[2])) :
         parts[0] == 'powers'    ? genPowers(_.parseInt(parts[2])) :
         parts[0] == 'evens'     ? genEvens() :
         parts[0] == 'odds'      ? genOdds() :
         parts[0] == 'interval'  ? genSetFromInterval(_.parseInt(parts[1]), _.parseInt(parts[2])) :
         null;
};
var learnConcept = function(examples, testQuery) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var rules = makeRuleHypothesisSpace();
    var intervals = makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(1, maxNumber);
    var hypothesis = flip(0.5) ? uniformDraw(rules) : uniformDraw(intervals);
    var set = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);
    mapData({data: examples}, function(example) {
      observe(Categorical({vs: set}), example);
    });
    return {hypothesis: hypothesis, testQueryResponse: inSet(testQuery, set)};
  });
};
End your program with the array pQueries of length 20: for each query 1..20, compute expectation(marginalize(learnConcept([3, 6, 9], query), function(x) { x.testQueryResponse })).
groundtruth code
var maxNumber = 20;
var filterByInRange =  function(set) {
  var inRange = function(v) {v <= maxNumber && v >= 0};
  return _.uniq(filter(inRange, set));
};
var genEvens = function() {
  return filter(function(v) {return v % 2 == 0}, _.range(1, maxNumber));
};
var genOdds = function() {
  return filter(function(v) {return (v + 1) % 2 == 0}, _.range(1, maxNumber));
};
var genMultiples = function(base) {
  var multiples = map(function(v) {return base * v}, _.range(maxNumber));
  return filterByInRange(multiples);
};
var genPowers = function(base) {
  var powers = map(function(v) {return Math.pow(base, v)}, _.range(maxNumber));
  return filterByInRange(powers);
};
var inSet = function(val, set) { return _.includes(set, val); };
var makeRuleHypothesisSpace = function() {
  var multipleRules = map(function(base) {return 'multiples_of_' + base}, _.range(1, 12));
  var powerRules = map(function(base) {return 'powers_of_' + base}, _.range(1, 12));
  return multipleRules.concat(powerRules).concat(['evens', 'odds']);
};
var genSetFromInterval = function(a, b) { return _.range(a, b+1); };
var makeIntervalHypothesisSpace = function(start, end) {
  var allIntervals = _.flatten(map(function(s) {
    return map(function(e) { [s, e] }, genSetFromInterval(s+1, end));
  }, genSetFromInterval(start, end)));
  return map(function(x) { 'interval_' + x[0] + '_' + x[1] }, allIntervals);
};
var getSetFromHypothesis = function(rule) {
  var parts = rule.split('_');
  return (parts[0] == 'multiples' ? genMultiples(_.parseInt(parts[2])) :
          parts[0] == 'powers' ? genPowers(_.parseInt(parts[2])) :
          parts[0] == 'evens' ? genEvens() :
          parts[0] == 'odds' ? genOdds() :
          parts[0] == 'interval' ? genSetFromInterval(_.parseInt(parts[1]), _.parseInt(parts[2])) :
          console.error('unknown rule' + rule));
};
var learnConcept = function(examples, testQuery) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var rules = makeRuleHypothesisSpace();
    var intervals = makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(1, maxNumber);
    var hypothesis = flip(0.5) ? uniformDraw(rules) : uniformDraw(intervals);
    var set = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);
    mapData({data: examples}, function(example) {
      observe(Categorical({vs: set}), example);
    });
    return {hypothesis: hypothesis,
            testQueryResponse: inSet(testQuery, set)};
  });
};

var examples = [3, 6, 9];
var queries = genSetFromInterval(1, maxNumber);
var ANSWER = (map(function(query) {
  var post = learnConcept(examples, query);
  return expectation(marginalize(post, function(x) { x.testQueryResponse }));
}, queries));

var maxNumber = 20;
var filterByInRange = function(set) {
  var inRange = function(v) { v <= maxNumber && v >= 0 };
  return _.uniq(filter(inRange, set));
};
var genEvens = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return v % 2 == 0 }, _.range(1, maxNumber));
};
var genOdds = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return (v + 1) % 2 == 0 }, _.range(1, maxNumber));
};
var genMultiples = function(base) {
  var multiples = map(function(v) { return base * v }, _.range(maxNumber));
  return filterByInRange(multiples);
};
var genPowers = function(base) {
  var powers = map(function(v) { return Math.pow(base, v) }, _.range(maxNumber));
  return filterByInRange(powers);
};
var inSet = function(val, set) { return _.includes(set, val); };
var makeRuleHypothesisSpace = function() {
  var multipleRules = map(function(b) { return 'multiples_of_' + b }, _.range(1, 12));
  var powerRules    = map(function(b) { return 'powers_of_' + b },    _.range(1, 12));
  return multipleRules.concat(powerRules).concat(['evens', 'odds']);
};
var genSetFromInterval = function(a, b) { return _.range(a, b+1); };
var makeIntervalHypothesisSpace = function(start, end) {
  var allIntervals = _.flatten(map(function(s) {
    return map(function(e) { return [s, e] }, genSetFromInterval(s+1, end));
  }, genSetFromInterval(start, end)));
  return map(function(x) { return 'interval_' + x[0] + '_' + x[1] }, allIntervals);
};
var getSetFromHypothesis = function(rule) {
  var parts = rule.split('_');
  return parts[0] == 'multiples' ? genMultiples(_.parseInt(parts[2])) :
         parts[0] == 'powers'    ? genPowers(_.parseInt(parts[2])) :
         parts[0] == 'evens'     ? genEvens() :
         parts[0] == 'odds'      ? genOdds() :
         parts[0] == 'interval'  ? genSetFromInterval(_.parseInt(parts[1]), _.parseInt(parts[2])) :
         null;
};
var learnConcept = function(examples, testQuery) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var rules = makeRuleHypothesisSpace();
    var intervals = makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(1, maxNumber);
    var hypothesis = flip(0.5) ? uniformDraw(rules) : uniformDraw(intervals);
    var set = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);
    mapData({data: examples}, function(example) {
      observe(Categorical({vs: set}), example);
    });
    return {hypothesis: hypothesis, testQueryResponse: inSet(testQuery, set)};
  });
};

var examples = [3, 6, 9];
var pQueries = map(function(query) {
  var dist = learnConcept(examples, query);
  var marginal = marginalize(dist, function(x) { return x.testQueryResponse; });
  return expectation(marginal);
}, _.range(1, 21));

var ANSWER = pQueries;
var maxNumber = 20;
var filterByInRange = function(set) {
  var inRange = function(v) { return v <= maxNumber && v >= 0; };
  return _.uniq(filter(inRange, set));
};
var genEvens = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return v % 2 == 0; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};
var genOdds = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return (v + 1) % 2 == 0; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};
var genMultiples = function(base) {
  var multiples = map(function(v) { return base * v; }, _.range(0, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(multiples);
};
var genPowers = function(base) {
  var powers = map(function(v) { return Math.pow(base, v); }, _.range(0, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(powers);
};
var inSet = function(val, set) { return _.includes(set, val); };
var makeRuleHypothesisSpace = function() {
  var multipleRules = map(function(b) { return 'multiples_of_' + b; }, _.range(1, 12));
  var powerRules = map(function(b) { return 'powers_of_' + b; }, _.range(1, 12));
  return multipleRules.concat(powerRules).concat(['evens', 'odds']);
};
var genSetFromInterval = function(a, b) { return _.range(a, b + 1); };
var makeIntervalHypothesisSpace = function(start, end) {
  var allIntervals = _.flatten(map(function(s) {
    return map(function(e) { return [s, e]; }, genSetFromInterval(s + 1, end));
  }, genSetFromInterval(start, end)));
  return map(function(x) { return 'interval_' + x[0] + '_' + x[1]; }, allIntervals);
};
var getSetFromHypothesis = function(rule) {
  var parts = rule.split('_');
  return parts[0] == 'multiples' ? genMultiples(_.parseInt(parts[2])) :
         parts[0] == 'powers' ? genPowers(_.parseInt(parts[2])) :
         parts[0] == 'evens' ? genEvens() :
         parts[0] == 'odds' ? genOdds() :
         parts[0] == 'interval' ? genSetFromInterval(_.parseInt(parts[1]), _.parseInt(parts[2])) :
         null;
};
var learnConcept = function(examples, testQuery) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var rules = makeRuleHypothesisSpace();
    var intervals = makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(1, maxNumber);
    var hypothesis = flip(0.5) ? uniformDraw(rules) : uniformDraw(intervals);
    var set = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);
    mapData({data: examples}, function(example) {
      observe(Categorical({vs: set}), example);
    });
    return {hypothesis: hypothesis, testQueryResponse: inSet(testQuery, set)};
  });
};

var examples = [3, 6, 9];
var pQueries = map(function(query) {
  var dist = learnConcept(examples, query);
  var marginal = marginalize(dist, function(x) { return x.testQueryResponse; });
  return expectation(marginal);
}, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));

var ANSWER = pQueries;
var maxNumber = 20;
var filterByInRange = function(set) {
  var inRange = function(v) { v <= maxNumber && v >= 0 };
  return _.uniq(filter(inRange, set));
};
var genEvens = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return v % 2 == 0 }, _.range(1, maxNumber));
};
var genOdds = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return (v + 1) % 2 == 0 }, _.range(1, maxNumber));
};
var genMultiples = function(base) {
  var multiples = map(function(v) { return base * v }, _.range(maxNumber));
  return filterByInRange(multiples);
};
var genPowers = function(base) {
  var powers = map(function(v) { return Math.pow(base, v) }, _.range(maxNumber));
  return filterByInRange(powers);
};
var inSet = function(val, set) { return _.includes(set, val); };
var makeRuleHypothesisSpace = function() {
  var multipleRules = map(function(b) { return 'multiples_of_' + b }, _.range(1, 12));
  var powerRules    = map(function(b) { return 'powers_of_' + b },    _.range(1, 12));
  return multipleRules.concat(powerRules).concat(['evens', 'odds']);
};
var genSetFromInterval = function(a, b) { return _.range(a, b+1); };
var makeIntervalHypothesisSpace = function(start, end) {
  var allIntervals = _.flatten(map(function(s) {
    return map(function(e) { return [s, e]; }, genSetFromInterval(s+1, end));
  }, genSetFromInterval(start, end)));
  return map(function(x) { return 'interval_' + x[0] + '_' + x[1]; }, allIntervals);
};
var getSetFromHypothesis = function(rule) {
  var parts = rule.split('_');
  return parts[0] == 'multiples' ? genMultiples(_.parseInt(parts[2])) :
         parts[0] == 'powers'    ? genPowers(_.parseInt(parts[2])) :
         parts[0] == 'evens'     ? genEvens() :
         parts[0] == 'odds'      ? genOdds() :
         parts[0] == 'interval'  ? genSetFromInterval(_.parseInt(parts[1]), _.parseInt(parts[2])) :
         null;
};
var learnConcept = function(examples, testQuery) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var rules = makeRuleHypothesisSpace();
    var intervals = makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(1, maxNumber);
    var hypothesis = flip(0.5) ? uniformDraw(rules) : uniformDraw(intervals);
    var set = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);
    mapData({data: examples}, function(example) {
      observe(Categorical({vs: set}), example);
    });
    return {hypothesis: hypothesis, testQueryResponse: inSet(testQuery, set)};
  });
};

var pQueries = map(function(query) {
  var dist = learnConcept([3, 6, 9], query);
  var marginal = marginalize(dist, function(x) { return x.testQueryResponse; });
  return expectation(marginal, function(x) { return x ? 1 : 0; });
}, _.range(1, 21));

var ANSWER = pQueries;
var maxNumber = 20;
var filterByInRange = function(set) {

  var powerRules    = map(function(b) { return 'powers_of_' + b; },    _.range(1, 12));

    var hypothesis = flip(0.5) ? uniformDraw(rules) : uniformDraw(intervals);
    var set = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);

      observe(Categorical({vs: set}), example);
    });
    return {hypothesis: hypothesis, testQueryResponse: inSet(testQuery, set)};
  });
};

};

  return filterByInRange(multiples);
};

  return filterByInRange(powers);

var inSet = function(val, set) { return _.includes(set, val); };
var makeRuleHypothesisSpace = function() {
  var multipleRules = map(function(b) { return 'multiples_of_' + b; }, _.range(1, 12));
  var powerRules    = map(function(b) { return 'powers_of_' + b; },    _.range(1, 12));
  return multipleRules.concat(powerRules).concat(['evens', 'odds']);
};
var genSetFromInterval = function(a, b) { return _.range(a, b + 1); };
var makeIntervalHypothesisSpace = function(start, end) {
  var allIntervals = _.flatten(map(function(s) {
    return map(function(e) { return [s, e]; }, genSetFromInterval(s + 1, end));
  }, genSetFromInterval(start, end)));
  return map(function(x) { return 'interval_' + x[0] + '_' + x[1]; }, allIntervals);

var getSetFromHypothesis = function(rule) {
  var parts = rule.split('_');
  return parts[0] == 'multiples' ? genMultiples(_.parseInt(parts[2])) :
         parts[0] == 'powers'    ? genPowers(_.parseInt(parts[2])) :
         parts[0] == 'evens'     ? genEvens() :

         parts[0] == 'interval'  ? genSetFromInterval(_.parseInt(parts[1]), _.parseInt(parts[2])) :

var learnConcept = function(examples, testQuery) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var rules = makeRuleHypothesisSpace();
    var intervals = makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(1, maxNumber);
    var hypothesis = flip(0.5) ? uniformDraw(rules) : uniformDraw(intervals);
    var set = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);
    mapData({data: examples}, function(example) {
      observe(Categorical({vs: set, ps: map(function(x) { return 1/set.length; }, set)}), example);
    });
    return {hypothesis: hypothesis, testQueryResponse: inSet(testQuery, set)};
  });
};

  var dist = learnConcept([3, 6, 9], query);
  var marginal = marginalize(dist, function(x) { return x.testQueryResponse; });
  return expectation(marginal);

    var intervals = makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(1, maxNumber);
    var hypothesis = flip(0.5) ? uniformDraw(rules) : uniformDraw(intervals);
    var set = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);
    mapData({data: examples}, function(example) {

var pQueries = map(function(query) {

  var marginal = marginalize(dist, function(x) { return x.testQueryResponse; });

var ANSWER = pQueries;
var maxNumber = 20;
var filterByInRange = function(set) {
  var inRange = function(v) { return v <= maxNumber && v >= 0; };
  return _.uniq(filter(inRange, set));
};
var genEvens = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return v % 2 == 0; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};
var genOdds = function() {
  return filter(function(v) { return (v + 1) % 2 == 0; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
};
var genMultiples = function(base) {
  var multiples = map(function(v) { return base * v; }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(multiples);
};
var genPowers = function(base) {
  var powers = map(function(v) { return Math.pow(base, v); }, _.range(1, maxNumber + 1));
  return filterByInRange(powers);
};
var inSet = function(val, set) { return _.includes(set, val); };
var makeRuleHypothesisSpace = function() {
  var multipleRules = map(function(b) { return 'multiples_of_' + b; }, _.range(1, 12));
  var powerRules    = map(function(b) { return 'powers_of_' + b; },    _.range(1, 12));
  return multipleRules.concat(powerRules).concat(['evens', 'odds']);
};
var genSetFromInterval = function(a, b) { return _.range(a, b + 1); };
var makeIntervalHypothesisSpace = function(start, end) {
  var allIntervals = _.flatten(map(function(s) {
    return map(function(e) { return [s, e]; }, genSetFromInterval(s + 1, end));
  }, genSetFromInterval(start, end - 1)));
  return map(function(x) { return 'interval_' + x[0] + '_' + x[1]; }, allIntervals);
};
var getSetFromHypothesis = function(rule) {
  var parts = rule.split('_');
  return parts[0] == 'multiples' ? genMultiples(_.parseInt(parts[2])) :
         parts[0] == 'powers'    ? genPowers(_.parseInt(parts[2])) :
         rule == 'evens'         ? genEvens() :
         rule == 'odds'          ? genOdds() :
         parts[0] == 'interval'  ? genSetFromInterval(_.parseInt(parts[1]), _.parseInt(parts[2])) :
         null;
};
var learnConcept = function(examples, testQuery) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var rules = makeRuleHypothesisSpace();
    var intervals = makeIntervalHypothesisSpace(1, maxNumber);
    var hypothesis = flip(0.5) ? uniformDraw(rules) : uniformDraw(intervals);
    var set = getSetFromHypothesis(hypothesis);
    mapData({data: examples}, function(example) {
      condition(inSet(example, set));
      factor(-Math.log(set.length));
    });
    return {hypothesis: hypothesis, testQueryResponse: inSet(testQuery, set)};
  });
};

var queries = _.range(1, 21);
var pQueries = map(function(query) {
  var dist = learnConcept([3, 6, 9], query);
  var margDist = marginalize(dist, function(x) { return x.testQueryResponse; });
  return expectation(margDist);
}, queries);

var ANSWER = pQueries;

probmods2-occams-razor/ex2.1exercisesdistribution8 runsTV=0.00TV=0
prompt
Given this Causal Power model where C is a possible cause of E: 
var observedData = [{C:true, E:false}];
Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 10000, lag:2}, function() {
  var cp = uniform(0, 1);  // Causal power of C to cause E
  var b = uniform(0, 1);   // Background probability of E
  mapData({data: observedData}, function(datum) {
    var E = (datum.C && flip(cp)) || flip(b);
    condition(E == datum.E);
  })
  return {cp, b};
})
Modify it into a Causal Support model: also infer whether there is a causal relation at all (relation = flip()). When relation is false, C does not affect E. Return {relation, cp, b}. End with the Infer(...).
groundtruth code
var observedData = [{C:true, E:false}];
var ANSWER = (Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 10000, lag:2}, function() {
  var relation = flip();
  var cp = uniform(0, 1);
  var b = uniform(0, 1);

  mapData({data: observedData}, function(datum) {
    var E = (relation && datum.C && flip(cp)) || flip(b);
    condition(E == datum.E);
  });

  return {relation, cp, b};
}));
generated code
haiku-45-noprimer-v3 TV=0haiku-45-primer-v3 TV=0haiku-45-think-noprimer-v3 TV<1haiku-45-think-primer-v3 TV<1sonnet-46-noprimer-v3 TV=0sonnet-46-primer-v3 TV=0sonnet-46-think-noprimer-v3 TV=0sonnet-46-think-primer-v3 TV=0
var observedData = [{C:true, E:false}];
var ANSWER = Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 10000, lag:2}, function() {
  var relation = flip();
  var cp = uniform(0, 1);  // Causal power of C to cause E
  var b = uniform(0, 1);   // Background probability of E
  mapData({data: observedData}, function(datum) {
    var E = (relation && datum.C && flip(cp)) || flip(b);
    condition(E == datum.E);
  })
  return {relation, cp, b};
});
var observedData = [{C:true, E:false}];
var ANSWER = Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 10000, lag:2}, function() {
  var relation = flip(0.5);  // Whether there is a causal relation
  var cp = uniform(0, 1);    // Causal power of C to cause E
  var b = uniform(0, 1);     // Background probability of E
  mapData({data: observedData}, function(datum) {
    var E = (relation && datum.C && flip(cp)) || flip(b);
    condition(E == datum.E);
  })
  return {relation, cp, b};
});
var observedData = [{C:true, E:false}];
Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 10000, lag:2}, function() {
  var relation = flip();  // Whether there is a causal relation
  var cp = uniform(0, 1);  // Causal power of C to cause E
  var b = uniform(0, 1);   // Background probability of E
  mapData({data: observedData}, function(datum) {
    var E = relation ? ((datum.C && flip(cp)) || flip(b)) : flip(b);
    condition(E == datum.E);
  })
  return {relation, cp, b};
})

  var b = uniform(0, 1);   // Background probability of E

  var b = uniform(0, 1);   // Background probability of E

});

probmods2-occams-razor/ex2.2exercisesdistribution8 runsTV=0.67TV<1
prompt
Given the Causal Support model (relation, cp, b latent; observedData = [{C:true, E:false}]; noisy-OR effect E = (relation && C && flip(cp)) || flip(b)): Single-site MH MCMC won't be efficient because changing relation alone rarely accepts. Improve efficiency by constructing the *marginal probability of E* directly via a small Infer, and use that in an observe (instead of sampling E and conditioning). Return {relation, cp, b}.
groundtruth code
var observedData = [{C:true, E:false}];
var ANSWER = (Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 10000, lag:2}, function() {
  var relation = flip();
  var cp = uniform(0, 1);
  var b = uniform(0, 1);

  var noisyOrMarginal = function(C) {
    return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
      return (relation && C && flip(cp)) || flip(b);
    });
  };

  mapData({data: observedData}, function(datum) {
    observe(noisyOrMarginal(datum.C), datum.E);
  });

  return {relation, cp, b};
}));
generated code
haiku-45-noprimer-v3 TV<1haiku-45-primer-v3 TV<1haiku-45-think-noprimer-v3 TV<1haiku-45-think-primer-v3 TV<1sonnet-46-noprimer-v3 TV<1sonnet-46-primer-v3 TV<1sonnet-46-think-noprimer-v3 TV<1sonnet-46-think-primer-v3 TV<1
// Causal Support model with improved efficiency
// Instead of sampling E and conditioning, we compute P(E|relation,cp,b) directly
// and use that in observe for better MCMC mixing

var model = function() {
  var relation = flip(0.5);
  var cp = uniform(0, 1);
  var b = uniform(0, 1);
  
  // Compute marginal probability of E given relation, cp, b
  // E = (relation && C && flip(cp)) || flip(b)
  // where C is observed to be true
  var probE = function(rel, c_prob, b_prob) {
    // C is true (observed)
    // E = (rel && true && flip(c_prob)) || flip(b_prob)
    // E = (rel && flip(c_prob)) || flip(b_prob)
    
    if (rel) {
      // E = flip(c_prob) || flip(b_prob)
      // P(E=true) = 1 - P(E=false) = 1 - (1-c_prob)*(1-b_prob)
      return 1 - (1 - c_prob) * (1 - b_prob);
    } else {
      // E = flip(b_prob)
      // P(E=true) = b_prob
      return b_prob;
    }
  };
  
  var pE = probE(relation, cp, b);
  
  // Observe E=false with probability (1 - pE)
  observe(Bernoulli({p: pE}), false);
  
  return {relation: relation, cp: cp, b: b};
};

var ANSWER = Infer({method: "MCMC", samples: 10000, lag: 10}, model);
var model = function() {
  var relation = flip(0.5);
  var cp = uniform(0, 1);
  var b = uniform(0, 1);
  
  // Marginal probability of E given relation, cp, b
  // E = (relation && C && flip(cp)) || flip(b), with C = true
  // P(E=false) = P(not relation) * P(flip(b)=false) + P(relation) * P(flip(cp)=false) * P(flip(b)=false)
  //            = (1-relation) * (1-b) + relation * (1-cp) * (1-b)
  //            = (1-b) * ((1-relation) + relation*(1-cp))
  //            = (1-b) * (1 - relation*cp)
  
  var pE_false = (1 - b) * (1 - (relation ? cp : 0));
  
  // Observe E = false
  observe(Bernoulli({p: 1 - pE_false}), false);
  
  return {relation: relation, cp: cp, b: b};
};

var ANSWER = Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 5000, burn: 1000}, model);
var model = function() {
  var relation = flip();
  var cp = uniform(0, 1);
  var b = uniform(0, 1);
  
  // Compute P(E = false | relation, cp, b, C = true) directly
  // E = (relation && C && flip(cp)) || flip(b)
  // With C = true, E = (relation && flip(cp)) || flip(b)
  // E = false iff both disjuncts are false:
  //   - (relation && flip(cp)) is false with prob: relation ? (1-cp) : 1
  //   - flip(b) is false with prob: (1-b)
  var probEFalse = function(relation, cp, b) {
    var pFirstDisjunctFalse = relation ? (1 - cp) : 1.0;
    var pSecondDisjunctFalse = 1 - b;
    return pFirstDisjunctFalse * pSecondDisjunctFalse;
  };
  
  // Observe E = false using the marginal probability
  // This avoids resampling E, making MH more efficient
  observe(Bernoulli({p: probEFalse(relation, cp, b)}), false);
  
  return {relation: relation, cp: cp, b: b};
};

var ANSWER = Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 5000}, model);
var model = function() {
  var relation = flip(0.5);
  var cp = uniform(0, 1);
  var b = uniform(0, 1);
  
  // Compute marginal probability of E given current latent values
  // by running a small inner inference that marginalizes over the stochastic flip()s
  var eDistribution = Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    // With C = true from observed data
    var E = (relation && flip(cp)) || flip(b);
    return E;
  });
  
  // Observe E = false directly against the marginal distribution
  // This is much more efficient than sampling E and conditioning,
  // because proposals to relation/cp/b are now scored by the exact marginal,
  // avoiding rejection from joint sampling
  observe(eDistribution, false);
  
  return {relation: relation, cp: cp, b: b};
};

var ANSWER = Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 10000}, model);
// Causal Support model with marginal likelihood approach
// observedData = [{C: true, E: false}]
// E = (relation && C && flip(cp)) || flip(b)  [noisy-OR]

var model = function() {
  // Sample latent variables
  var relation = flip(0.5);
  var cp = uniform(0, 1);
  var b = uniform(0, 1);
  
  // For each observation, compute marginal P(E | C, relation, cp, b)
  // and use factor/observe instead of sampling E
  
  // Observation: C = true, E = false
  var C = true;
  var E_obs = false;
  
  // Compute P(E = true | C, relation, cp, b) analytically
  // E = (relation && C && flip(cp)) || flip(b)
  // Using noisy-OR:
  // P(E=1) = 1 - P(E=0)
  // P(E=0) = P(not from causal path) * P(not from background)
  // P(not from background) = (1 - b)
  // P(not from causal path) = if (relation && C) then (1 - cp) else 1
  
  var p_causal_fires = (relation && C) ? cp : 0;
  var p_E_true = 1 - (1 - p_causal_fires) * (1 - b);
  var p_E_false = 1 - p_E_true;
  
  // Observe E = false
  var p_E_obs = E_obs ? p_E_true : p_E_false;
  factor(Math.log(p_E_obs));
  
  return {relation: relation, cp: cp, b: b};
};

var ANSWER = Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 50000, burn: 5000, kernel: 'MH'}, model);
var model = function() {
  var relation = flip(0.5);
  var cp = uniform(0, 1);
  var b = uniform(0, 1);

  // Compute marginal probability of E given C=true using a small inner Infer
  var eDistGivenCTrue = Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var E = (relation && flip(cp)) || flip(b);
    return E;
  });

  // Observed data: {C: true, E: false}
  // P(E=false | C=true, relation, cp, b)
  observe(eDistGivenCTrue, false);

  return {relation: relation, cp: cp, b: b};
};

var ANSWER = Infer({
  method: 'MCMC',
  samples: 10000,
  burn: 2000,
  kernel: 'MH'
}, model);
// Causal Support model with marginalised E for efficient MCMC
// Observed: [{C:true, E:false}]
// Noisy-OR: E = (relation && C && flip(cp)) || flip(b)

var model = function() {
  // Priors over latent structure and parameters
  var relation = flip(0.5);
  var cp       = uniform(0, 1);   // causal power
  var b        = uniform(0, 1);   // background rate

  // For each observation, build a *small enumeration* over E
  // to get P(E | relation, cp, b, C) analytically, then observe.
  map(function(obs) {
    var C = obs.C;
    var observedE = obs.E;

    // Marginal distribution of E under noisy-OR given current latents
    var eDist = Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
      var causalFires = relation && C && flip(cp);
      var bgFires     = flip(b);
      return causalFires || bgFires;
    });

    // Soft-condition on the observed E value
    observe(eDist, observedE);
  }, [{C: true, E: false}]);

  return {relation: relation, cp: cp, b: b};
};

var ANSWER = Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 20000, burn: 2000, lag: 2}, model);
var observedData = [{C: true, E: false}];

// Compute P(E=true | C, relation, cp, b) by marginalising out the internal flips
// via a nested enumerate inference — this avoids ever sampling E and conditioning on it.
var marginalPofE = function(C, relation, cp, b) {
  var dist = Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var causalPath = relation && C && flip(cp);
    var background  = flip(b);
    return causalPath || background;   // noisy-OR
  });
  return Math.exp(dist.score(true));  // P(E = true)
};

var model = function() {
  // Priors
  var relation = flip(0.5);
  var cp = uniform(0, 1);
  var b  = uniform(0, 1);

  // Soft-condition on each observation via the marginal likelihood of E
  mapData({data: observedData}, function(datum) {
    var pE = marginalPofE(datum.C, relation, cp, b);
    // observe E_observed under Bernoulli(pE) — no hard condition needed
    observe(Bernoulli({p: pE}), datum.E);
  });

  return {relation: relation, cp: cp, b: b};
};

var ANSWER = Infer(
  {method: 'MCMC', samples: 20000, lag: 5, burn: 2000,
   kernel: {MH: {}}},
  model
);

prompt
Compare Causal Power (CP) and Causal Support (CS) models on data where E occurs varying amounts with and without C. 
var generateData = function(numEWithC, numEWithoutC) {
  var eWithC = repeat(numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: true}});
  var noEWithC = repeat(8 - numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: false}});
  var eWithoutC = repeat(numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: true}});
  var noEWithoutC = repeat(8 - numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: false}});
  return _.flatten([eWithC, noEWithC, eWithoutC, noEWithoutC]);
}
var dataParams = [[8,8],[6,6],[4,4],[2,2],[0,0],[8,6],[6,4],[4,2],[2,0],[8,4],[6,2],[4,0],[8,2],[6,0],[8,0]];
Implement cpPost(observedData) (returning posterior over cp) and csPost(observedData) (returning posterior over relation*cp), both using the marginal-noisy-OR observe from Ex 2.2. Return an object literal {cpValues, csValues} where each is an array of expectation(...) of the corresponding posterior across all 15 dataParams configurations.
groundtruth code
var generateData = function(numEWithC, numEWithoutC) {
  var eWithC = repeat(numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: true}});
  var noEWithC = repeat(8 - numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: false}});
  var eWithoutC = repeat(numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: true}});
  var noEWithoutC = repeat(8 - numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: false}});
  return _.flatten([eWithC, noEWithC, eWithoutC, noEWithoutC]);
};

var dataParams = [[8, 8], [6, 6], [4, 4], [2, 2], [0, 0], [8, 6],
                  [6, 4], [4, 2], [2, 0], [8, 4], [6, 2], [4, 0],
                  [8, 2], [6, 0], [8, 0]];

var data = map(function(x) { generateData(x[0], x[1]) }, dataParams);

var cpPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', burn: 2000, samples: 1000, lag:2}, function() {
    var cp = uniform(0, 1);
    var b = uniform(0, 1);
    var noisyOrMarginal = function(C) {
      return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
        return (C && flip(cp)) || flip(b);
      });
    };
    mapData({data: observedData}, function(datum) {
      observe(noisyOrMarginal(datum.C), datum.E);
    });
    return cp;
  });
};

var csPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', burn: 2000, samples: 1000, lag:2}, function() {
    var relation = flip();
    var cp = uniform(0, 1);
    var b = uniform(0, 1);
    var noisyOrMarginal = function(C) {
      return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
        return (relation && C && flip(cp)) || flip(b);
      });
    };
    mapData({data: observedData}, function(datum) {
      observe(noisyOrMarginal(datum.C), datum.E);
    });
    return relation * cp;
  });
};
var ANSWER = (({
  cpValues: map(function(d) { expectation(cpPost(d)) }, data),
  csValues: map(function(d) { expectation(csPost(d)) }, data)
}));
generated code
haiku-45-noprimer-v3 val-haiku-45-primer-v3 failhaiku-45-think-noprimer-v3 failhaiku-45-think-primer-v3 failsonnet-46-noprimer-v3 val-sonnet-46-primer-v3 val-sonnet-46-think-noprimer-v3 val-sonnet-46-think-primer-v3 val-
var generateData = function(numEWithC, numEWithoutC) {
  var eWithC = repeat(numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: true}});
  var noEWithC = repeat(8 - numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: false}});
  var eWithoutC = repeat(numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: true}});
  var noEWithoutC = repeat(8 - numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: false}});
  return _.flatten([eWithC, noEWithC, eWithoutC, noEWithoutC]);
}

var dataParams = [[8,8],[6,6],[4,4],[2,2],[0,0],[8,6],[6,4],[4,2],[2,0],[8,4],[6,2],[4,0],[8,2],[6,0],[8,0]];

// Marginal noisy-OR model from Ex 2.2
var noisyOrModel = function(cp, baseRate) {
  return function(c) {
    var pE = c ? (1 - (1 - baseRate) * (1 - cp)) : baseRate;
    return Bernoulli({p: pE});
  };
};

// Causal Power posterior
var cpPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 2000}, function() {
    var cp = uniform(0, 1);
    var baseRate = uniform(0, 1);
    var model = noisyOrModel(cp, baseRate);
    
    mapData({data: observedData}, function(datum) {
      observe(model(datum.C), datum.E);
    });
    
    return cp;
  });
};

// Causal Support posterior (relation * cp)
var csPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 2000}, function() {
    var cp = uniform(0, 1);
    var baseRate = uniform(0, 1);
    var model = noisyOrModel(cp, baseRate);
    
    mapData({data: observedData}, function(datum) {
      observe(model(datum.C), datum.E);
    });
    
    // Causal Support: relation * cp
    // relation = P(E|C) - P(E|¬C)
    var pEC = 1 - (1 - baseRate) * (1 - cp);
    var pEnotC = baseRate;
    var relation = pEC - pEnotC;
    var cs = relation * cp;
    
    return cs;
  });
};

// Compute expectations across all data configurations
var cpValues = map(function(params) {
  var data = generateData(params[0], params[1]);
  var post = cpPost(data);
  return expectation(post);
}, dataParams);

var csValues = map(function(params) {
  var data = generateData(params[0], params[1]);
  var post = csPost(data);
  return expectation(post);
}, dataParams);

var ANSWER = {cpValues: cpValues, csValues: csValues};

var generateData = function(numEWithC, numEWithoutC) {
  var eWithC = repeat(numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: true}});
  var noEWithC = repeat(8 - numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: false}});
  var eWithoutC = repeat(numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: true}});
  var noEWithoutC = repeat(8 - numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: false}});
  return _.flatten([eWithC, noEWithC, eWithoutC, noEWithoutC]);
};

var dataParams = [[8,8],[6,6],[4,4],[2,2],[0,0],[8,6],[6,4],[4,2],[2,0],[8,4],[6,2],[4,0],[8,2],[6,0],[8,0]];

// Marginal-noisy-OR model from Ex 2.2
var cpPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 5000}, function() {
    var cp = uniform(0, 1);
    var baseRate = uniform(0, 1);
    
    mapData({data: observedData}, function(datum) {
      var pE = datum.C ? (1 - (1 - baseRate) * (1 - cp)) : baseRate;
      observe(Bernoulli({p: pE}), datum.E ? 1 : 0);
    });
    
    return cp;
  });
};

var csPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 5000}, function() {
    var cp = uniform(0, 1);
    var baseRate = uniform(0, 1);
    var relation = uniform(0, 1);
    
    mapData({data: observedData}, function(datum) {
      var pE = datum.C ? (1 - (1 - baseRate) * (1 - relation * cp)) : baseRate;
      observe(Bernoulli({p: pE}), datum.E ? 1 : 0);
    });
    
    return relation * cp;
  });
};

var cpValues = map(function(params) {
  var data = generateData(params[0], params[1]);
  var post = cpPost(data);
  return expectation(post);
}, dataParams);

var csValues = map(function(params) {
  var data = generateData(params[0], params[1]);
  var post = csPost(data);
  return expectation(post);
}, dataParams);

var ANSWER = {cpValues: cpValues, csValues: csValues};
Timeout after 60s
var generateData = function(numEWithC, numEWithoutC) {
  var eWithC = repeat(numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: true}});
  var noEWithC = repeat(8 - numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: false}});
  var eWithoutC = repeat(numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: true}});
  var noEWithoutC = repeat(8 - numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: false}});
  return _.flatten([eWithC, noEWithC, eWithoutC, noEWithoutC]);
};

var cpPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 1000}, function() {
    var cp = uniform(0, 1);
    var bg = uniform(0, 1);
    
    mapData({data: observedData}, function(datum) {
      var probE = datum.C ? (bg + (1 - bg) * cp) : bg;
      observe(Bernoulli({p: probE}), datum.E ? 1 : 0);
    });
    
    return cp;
  });
};

var csPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 1000}, function() {
    var cp = uniform(0, 1);
    var relation = uniform(0, 1);
    var bg = uniform(0, 1);
    
    mapData({data: observedData}, function(datum) {
      var probE = datum.C ? 
        (bg + (1 - bg) * relation * cp) : 
        bg;
      observe(Bernoulli({p: probE}), datum.E ? 1 : 0);
    });
    
    return relation * cp;
  });
};

var dataParams = [[8,8],[6,6],[4,4],[2,2],[0,0],[8,6],[6,4],[4,2],[2,0],[8,4],[6,2],[4,0],[8,2],[6,0],[8,0]];

var cpValues = map(function(params) {
  var data = generateData(params[0], params[1]);
  return expectation(cpPost(data));
}, dataParams);

var csValues = map(function(params) {
  var data = generateData(params[0], params[1]);
  return expectation(csPost(data));
}, dataParams);

var ANSWER = {cpValues: cpValues, csValues: csValues};
Timeout after 60s
var generateData = function(numEWithC, numEWithoutC) {
  var eWithC = repeat(numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: true}});
  var noEWithC = repeat(8 - numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: false}});
  var eWithoutC = repeat(numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: true}});
  var noEWithoutC = repeat(8 - numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: false}});
  return _.flatten([eWithC, noEWithC, eWithoutC, noEWithoutC]);
};

var cpPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 2000}, function() {
    // Priors
    var cp = beta(1, 1);  // Causal power, uniform prior
    var b = beta(1, 1);   // Base rate (alternative causes)
    
    // Noisy-OR likelihood
    mapData({data: observedData}, function(datum) {
      var pE = datum.C ? (cp + (1 - cp) * b) : b;
      observe(Bernoulli({p: pE}), datum.E ? 1 : 0);
    });
    
    return cp;
  });
};

var csPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 2000}, function() {
    // Priors
    var cp = beta(1, 1);
    var b = beta(1, 1);
    
    // Noisy-OR likelihood
    mapData({data: observedData}, function(datum) {
      var pE = datum.C ? (cp + (1 - cp) * b) : b;
      observe(Bernoulli({p: pE}), datum.E ? 1 : 0);
    });
    
    // Causal Support = relation * cp
    // relation measures how much E is more likely given C vs. without C
    var pEC = cp + (1 - cp) * b;
    var pNotEC = b;
    var relation = (pEC - pNotEC) / Math.max(1 - pNotEC, 0.001);
    
    return relation * cp;
  });
};

var dataParams = [[8,8],[6,6],[4,4],[2,2],[0,0],[8,6],[6,4],[4,2],[2,0],[8,4],[6,2],[4,0],[8,2],[6,0],[8,0]];

var cpValues = map(function(params) {
  var data = generateData(params[0], params[1]);
  var post = cpPost(data);
  return expectation(post);
}, dataParams);

var csValues = map(function(params) {
  var data = generateData(params[0], params[1]);
  var post = csPost(data);
  return expectation(post);
}, dataParams);

var ANSWER = {cpValues: cpValues, csValues: csValues};
// Marginal noisy-OR likelihood
// P(E=1 | C, cp, b) = 1 - (1-b) * (C ? (1-cp) : 1)
// We marginalize over b with a uniform prior

var noiseORObserve = function(datum, cp, b) {
  var pE = datum.C ? (1 - (1 - b) * (1 - cp)) : b;
  return datum.E ? Math.log(pE) : Math.log(1 - pE);
};

var cpPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 5000, burn: 1000}, function() {
    var cp = uniform(0, 1);
    var b = uniform(0, 1);
    
    var logLik = sum(map(function(datum) {
      return noiseORObserve(datum, cp, b);
    }, observedData));
    
    factor(logLik);
    
    return cp;
  });
};

var csPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 5000, burn: 1000}, function() {
    var relation = flip(0.5) ? 1 : 0; // causal relation exists or not
    var cp = uniform(0, 1);
    var b = uniform(0, 1);
    
    // If no relation, cp effectively = 0 (C has no causal power)
    var effectiveCp = relation === 1 ? cp : 0;
    
    var logLik = sum(map(function(datum) {
      return noiseORObserve(datum, effectiveCp, b);
    }, observedData));
    
    factor(logLik);
    
    return relation * cp;
  });
};

var generateData = function(numEWithC, numEWithoutC) {
  var eWithC = repeat(numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: true}});
  var noEWithC = repeat(8 - numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: false}});
  var eWithoutC = repeat(numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: true}});
  var noEWithoutC = repeat(8 - numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: false}});
  return _.flatten([eWithC, noEWithC, eWithoutC, noEWithoutC]);
};

var dataParams = [[8,8],[6,6],[4,4],[2,2],[0,0],[8,6],[6,4],[4,2],[2,0],[8,4],[6,2],[4,0],[8,2],[6,0],[8,0]];

var cpValues = map(function(params) {
  var data = generateData(params[0], params[1]);
  var post = cpPost(data);
  return expectation(post);
}, dataParams);

var csValues = map(function(params) {
  var data = generateData(params[0], params[1]);
  var post = csPost(data);
  return expectation(post);
}, dataParams);

var ANSWER = {cpValues: cpValues, csValues: csValues};
var cpPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 5000, burn: 1000}, function() {
    var b = uniform(0, 1);
    var cp = uniform(0, 1);
    
    mapData({data: observedData}, function(d) {
      var pE = d.C ? (1 - (1 - b) * (1 - cp)) : b;
      observe(Bernoulli({p: pE}), d.E);
    });
    
    return cp;
  });
};

var csPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 5000, burn: 1000}, function() {
    var b = uniform(0, 1);
    var relation = flip(0.5);
    var cp = relation ? uniform(0, 1) : 0;
    
    mapData({data: observedData}, function(d) {
      var pE = d.C ? (1 - (1 - b) * (1 - cp)) : b;
      observe(Bernoulli({p: pE}), d.E);
    });
    
    return relation * cp;
  });
};

var generateData = function(numEWithC, numEWithoutC) {
  var eWithC = repeat(numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: true}});
  var noEWithC = repeat(8 - numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: false}});
  var eWithoutC = repeat(numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: true}});
  var noEWithoutC = repeat(8 - numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: false}});
  return _.flatten([eWithC, noEWithC, eWithoutC, noEWithoutC]);
};

var dataParams = [[8,8],[6,6],[4,4],[2,2],[0,0],[8,6],[6,4],[4,2],[2,0],[8,4],[6,2],[4,0],[8,2],[6,0],[8,0]];

var allData = map(function(params) {
  return generateData(params[0], params[1]);
}, dataParams);

var cpValues = map(function(data) {
  var post = cpPost(data);
  return expectation(post);
}, allData);

var csValues = map(function(data) {
  var post = csPost(data);
  return expectation(post);
}, allData);

var ANSWER = {cpValues: cpValues, csValues: csValues};
var generateData = function(numEWithC, numEWithoutC) {
  var eWithC = repeat(numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: true}});
  var noEWithC = repeat(8 - numEWithC, function() {return {C: true, E: false}});
  var eWithoutC = repeat(numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: true}});
  var noEWithoutC = repeat(8 - numEWithoutC, function() {return {C: false, E: false}});
  return _.flatten([eWithC, noEWithC, eWithoutC, noEWithoutC]);
};

var dataParams = [[8,8],[6,6],[4,4],[2,2],[0,0],[8,6],[6,4],[4,2],[2,0],[8,4],[6,2],[4,0],[8,2],[6,0],[8,0]];

// Marginal noisy-OR observe from Ex 2.2
var marginalNoisyORObs = function(data, b, cp) {
  mapData({data: data}, function(d) {
    var pE = d.C ? (b + cp - b * cp) : b;
    var pEclamped = Math.max(1e-10, Math.min(1 - 1e-10, pE));
    factor(d.E ? Math.log(pEclamped) : Math.log(1 - pEclamped));
  });
};

// CP model: infer causal power cp (with background b marginalized)
var cpPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 10000, burn: 2000, lag: 2}, function() {
    var b  = uniform(0, 1);
    var cp = uniform(0, 1);
    marginalNoisyORObs(observedData, b, cp);
    return cp;
  });
};

// CS model: infer relation (binary) * cp, capturing causal support
var csPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 10000, burn: 2000, lag: 2}, function() {
    var b        = uniform(0, 1);
    var relation = flip(0.5);
    var cp       = uniform(0, 1);
    // if no causal relation, effective cp is 0
    var effectiveCp = relation ? cp : 0;
    marginalNoisyORObs(observedData, b, effectiveCp);
    return relation ? cp : 0;
  });
};

var cpValues = map(function(params) {
  var data = generateData(params[0], params[1]);
  return expectation(cpPost(data));
}, dataParams);

var csValues = map(function(params) {
  var data = generateData(params[0], params[1]);
  return expectation(csPost(data));
}, dataParams);

var ANSWER = {cpValues: cpValues, csValues: csValues};
var generateData = function(numEWithC, numEWithoutC) {
  var eWithC = repeat(numEWithC, function() { return {C: true, E: true}; });
  var noEWithC = repeat(8 - numEWithC, function() { return {C: true, E: false}; });
  var eWithoutC = repeat(numEWithoutC, function() { return {C: false, E: true}; });
  var noEWithoutC = repeat(8 - numEWithoutC, function() { return {C: false, E: false}; });
  return _.flatten([eWithC, noEWithC, eWithoutC, noEWithoutC]);
};

var dataParams = [[8,8],[6,6],[4,4],[2,2],[0,0],[8,6],[6,4],[4,2],[2,0],[8,4],[6,2],[4,0],[8,2],[6,0],[8,0]];

// Causal Power: posterior over cp using noisy-OR marginal observe
var cpPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 10000, burn: 2000}, function() {
    var b = beta(1, 1);
    var cp = beta(1, 1);

    mapData({data: observedData}, function(d) {
      var pE = d.C ? (1 - (1 - b) * (1 - cp)) : b;
      observe(Bernoulli({p: pE}), d.E);
    });

    return cp;
  });
};

// Causal Support: posterior over relation*cp using noisy-OR marginal observe
var csPost = function(observedData) {
  return Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 10000, burn: 2000}, function() {
    var b = beta(1, 1);
    var relation = flip(0.5);  // binary: does C have causal relation to E?
    var cp = beta(1, 1);

    var effectiveCp = relation ? cp : 0;

    mapData({data: observedData}, function(d) {
      var pE = d.C ? (1 - (1 - b) * (1 - effectiveCp)) : b;
      observe(Bernoulli({p: pE}), d.E);
    });

    return relation * cp;
  });
};

var allData = map(function(params) {
  return generateData(params[0], params[1]);
}, dataParams);

var cpValues = map(function(data) {
  return expectation(cpPost(data));
}, allData);

var csValues = map(function(data) {
  return expectation(csPost(data));
}, allData);

var ANSWER = {cpValues: cpValues, csValues: csValues};
