exercisessocial-cognition6 atoms5✓ · 0✗
probmods2-social-cognition/ex1.1exercisesdistribution8 runsTV=0.00TV=0
prompt
Sally chooses an action 'a', 'b', or 'c' that maps to a vending machine outcome (each action gives 80% chance of one specific food, 10% each for the others). If Sally is *deceptive* she chooses an action that does NOT lead to her goal food; otherwise she chooses one that does. Fill in the condition(...) calls. End with the Infer(...) call returning the posterior over Sally's goal food given that she is deceptive AND chose 'b'. 
var actionPrior = Categorical({vs: ['a','b','c'], ps: [1/3,1/3,1/3]});
var foodPrior = Categorical({vs: ['bagel','cookie','doughnut'], ps: [1/3,1/3,1/3]});
var vendingMachine = /* maps action -> categorical food */;

var chooseAction = function(goal, transition, state, deceive) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var action = sample(actionPrior);
    condition(...)
    return action;
  })
};

Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
  var deceive = flip();
  var goalFood = sample(foodPrior);
  var goal = function(outcome) {return outcome == goalFood};
  var sallyActionDist = chooseAction(goal, vendingMachine, 'state', deceive);
  condition(...)
  return goalFood;
});
groundtruth code
var actionPrior = Categorical({vs: ['a', 'b', 'c'], ps: [1/3, 1/3, 1/3]});
var foodPrior = Categorical({vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut'], ps: [1/3, 1/3, 1/3]});

var vendingMachine = function(state, action) {
  return action == 'a' ? categorical({vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut'], ps: [.8, .1, .1]}) :
         action == 'b' ? categorical({vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut'], ps: [.1, .8, .1]}) :
         action == 'c' ? categorical({vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut'], ps: [.1, .1, .8]}) :
         'nothing';
};

var chooseAction = function(goal, transition, state, deceive) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var action = sample(actionPrior);
    var outcome = transition(state, action);
    condition(deceive ? !goal(outcome) : goal(outcome));
    return action;
  });
};
var ANSWER = (Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
  var deceive = flip();
  var goalFood = sample(foodPrior);
  var goal = function(outcome) {return outcome == goalFood};
  var sallyActionDist = chooseAction(goal, vendingMachine, 'state', deceive);
  condition(deceive);
  condition(sample(sallyActionDist) == 'b');
  return goalFood;
}));

var foodPrior = Categorical({vs: ['bagel','cookie','doughnut'], ps: [1/3,1/3,1/3]});

var vendingMachine = function(action) {
  var actionToFood = {
    'a': 'bagel',
    'b': 'cookie',
    'c': 'doughnut'
  };
  var goalFood = actionToFood[action];

      goalFood === 'doughnut' ? 0.8 : 0.1
    ]
  });
};

  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var action = sample(actionPrior);
    var outcome = sample(transition(action));
    var goalAchieved = goal(outcome);
    if (deceive) {
      condition(!goalAchieved);
    } else {
      condition(goalAchieved);
    }
    return action;
  })
};

var actionPrior = Categorical({vs: ['a','b','c'], ps: [1/3,1/3,1/3]});
var foodPrior = Categorical({vs: ['bagel','cookie','doughnut'], ps: [1/3,1/3,1/3]});

var vendingMachine = function(action) {
  var actionToFood = {
    'a': 'bagel',
    'b': 'cookie',
    'c': 'doughnut'
  };
  var goalFood = actionToFood[action];
  return Categorical({
    vs: ['bagel','cookie','doughnut'],
    ps: (function() {
      var ps = [0, 0, 0];
      var foodIndex = {'bagel': 0, 'cookie': 1, 'doughnut': 2}[goalFood];
      ps[foodIndex] = 0.8;
      var others = _.range(3).filter(function(i) { return i !== foodIndex; });
      ps[others[0]] = 0.1;
      ps[others[1]] = 0.1;
      return ps;
    })()
  });
};

var chooseAction = function(goal, transition, state, deceive) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var action = sample(actionPrior);
    var outcome = sample(transition(action));
    var goalAchieved = goal(outcome);

  });
};

var foodPrior = Categorical({vs: ['bagel','cookie','doughnut'], ps: [1/3,1/3,1/3]});

var vendingMachine = function(action) {
  if (action === 'a') {
    return Categorical({vs: ['bagel','cookie','doughnut'], ps: [0.8, 0.1, 0.1]});
  } else if (action === 'b') {
    return Categorical({vs: ['bagel','cookie','doughnut'], ps: [0.1, 0.8, 0.1]});
  } else {

  }
};

var actionToMainFood = function(action) {
  if (action === 'a') return 'bagel';
  if (action === 'b') return 'cookie';
  return 'doughnut';
};

var chooseAction = function(goal, transition, state, deceive) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var action = sample(actionPrior);
    var actionLeadsToGoal = goal(actionToMainFood(action));
    condition(deceive ? !actionLeadsToGoal : actionLeadsToGoal);
    return action;
  })
};

    ps: action === 'a' ? [0.8, 0.1, 0.1] :

  });

var chooseAction = function(goal, transition, state, deceive) {

    var action = sample(actionPrior);
    var outcome = sample(transition(action));
    condition(deceive ? !goal(outcome) : goal(outcome));

var actionPrior = Categorical({vs: ['a','b','c'], ps: [1/3,1/3,1/3]});
var foodPrior   = Categorical({vs: ['bagel','cookie','doughnut'], ps: [1/3,1/3,1/3]});

// action 'a' → mostly bagel, 'b' → mostly cookie, 'c' → mostly doughnut
var vendingMachine = function(action) {
  if (action === 'a') {
    return Categorical({vs: ['bagel','cookie','doughnut'], ps: [0.8, 0.1, 0.1]});
  } else if (action === 'b') {
    return Categorical({vs: ['bagel','cookie','doughnut'], ps: [0.1, 0.8, 0.1]});
  } else {
    return Categorical({vs: ['bagel','cookie','doughnut'], ps: [0.1, 0.1, 0.8]});
  }
};

// chooseAction: if deceive, pick an action whose outcome does NOT satisfy goal;
//               otherwise pick one that does.
var chooseAction = function(goal, transition, state, deceive) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var action  = sample(actionPrior);
    var outcome = sample(transition(action));
    condition(deceive ? !goal(outcome) : goal(outcome));
    return action;
  });
};

var ANSWER = Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
  var deceive    = flip();
  var goalFood   = sample(foodPrior);
  var goal       = function(outcome) { return outcome === goalFood; };
  var sallyActionDist = chooseAction(goal, vendingMachine, 'state', deceive);

  // Observe: Sally IS deceptive AND chose action 'b'
  condition(deceive === true);
  condition(sample(sallyActionDist) === 'b');

  return goalFood;
});
var actionPrior = Categorical({vs: ['a','b','c'], ps: [1/3,1/3,1/3]});
var foodPrior = Categorical({vs: ['bagel','cookie','doughnut'], ps: [1/3,1/3,1/3]});

var vendingMachine = function(action) {
  return Categorical({
    vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut'],
    ps: action == 'a' ? [0.8, 0.1, 0.1] :
        action == 'b' ? [0.1, 0.8, 0.1] :
                        [0.1, 0.1, 0.8]
  });
};

var chooseAction = function(goal, transition, state, deceive) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var action = sample(actionPrior);
    // If deceiving, pick an action whose outcome does NOT satisfy goal;
    // otherwise pick one whose outcome DOES satisfy goal.
    condition(deceive
      ? !goal(sample(transition(action)))
      :  goal(sample(transition(action))));
    return action;
  });
};

var ANSWER = Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
  var deceive = flip();
  var goalFood = sample(foodPrior);
  var goal = function(outcome) { return outcome == goalFood; };
  var sallyActionDist = chooseAction(goal, vendingMachine, 'state', deceive);
  // Condition on Sally being deceptive AND having chosen action 'b'
  condition(deceive);
  condition(sample(sallyActionDist) == 'b');
  return goalFood;
});

probmods2-social-cognition/ex1.2exercisesdistribution8 runsTV=0.06TV<.5
prompt
Sally chooses 'a', 'b', or 'c'. If deceptive she avoids her goal food; otherwise she picks an action whose outcome IS her goal food. The vending machine: action 'a' -> 80% bagel, 'b' -> 80% cookie, 'c' -> 80% doughnut (10% each for the others). You observe Sally choose 'b' twice in a row. End with the Infer(...) returning the posterior over her goal food.
groundtruth code
var actionPrior = Categorical({vs: ['a', 'b', 'c'], ps: [1/3, 1/3, 1/3]});
var foodPrior = Categorical({vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut'], ps: [1/3, 1/3, 1/3]});

var vendingMachine = function(state, action) {
  return action == 'a' ? categorical({vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut'], ps: [.8, .1, .1]}) :
         action == 'b' ? categorical({vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut'], ps: [.1, .8, .1]}) :
         action == 'c' ? categorical({vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut'], ps: [.1, .1, .8]}) :
         'nothing';
};

var chooseAction = function(goal, transition, state, deceive) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var action = sample(actionPrior);
    var outcome = transition(state, action);
    condition(deceive ? !goal(outcome) : goal(outcome));
    return action;
  });
};
var ANSWER = (Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
  var deceive = flip();
  var goalFood = sample(foodPrior);
  var goal = function(outcome) {return outcome == goalFood};
  var sallyActionDist = chooseAction(goal, vendingMachine, 'state', deceive);
  condition(sample(sallyActionDist) == 'b');
  condition(sample(sallyActionDist) == 'b');
  return goalFood;
}));

Error: sample() expected a distribution but received "false".
var model = function() {
  var goal = uniformDraw(['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut']);
  var deceptive = flip(0.5);
  
  var actionDist = function(goal, deceptive) {
    if (deceptive) {
      // Avoid goal food: choose action that doesn't give goal with high probability
      if (goal === 'bagel') return uniformDraw(['b', 'c']);
      if (goal === 'cookie') return uniformDraw(['a', 'c']);
      if (goal === 'doughnut') return uniformDraw(['a', 'b']);
    } else {
      // Pick action whose outcome IS goal food
      if (goal === 'bagel') return 'a';
      if (goal === 'cookie') return 'b';
      if (goal === 'doughnut') return 'c';
    }
  };
  
  var outcomeDist = function(action) {
    if (action === 'a') return categorical({ps: [0.8, 0.1, 0.1], vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut']});
    if (action === 'b') return categorical({ps: [0.1, 0.8, 0.1], vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut']});
    if (action === 'c') return categorical({ps: [0.1, 0.1, 0.8], vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut']});
  };
  
  // Sally chooses 'b' twice
  var action1 = actionDist(goal, deceptive);
  observe(action1 === 'b');
  
  var action2 = actionDist(goal, deceptive);
  observe(action2 === 'b');
  
  return goal;
};

var ANSWER = Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, model);
var model = function() {
  // Sally's goal food
  var goalFood = uniformDrift({a: 0, b: 1, width: 0.25});
  var goal = categorical({vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut'], ps: [goalFood, 1-goalFood, 0]});
  
  // Sally's deceptiveness
  var deceptive = flip(0.5);
  
  // Vending machine outcomes given action
  var machineOutcome = function(action) {
    var outcomes = ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut'];
    var probs = (action === 'a') ? [0.8, 0.1, 0.1] :
                (action === 'b') ? [0.1, 0.8, 0.1] :
                (action === 'c') ? [0.1, 0.1, 0.8] :
                [1/3, 1/3, 1/3];
    return categorical({vs: outcomes, ps: probs});
  };
  
  // Sally's action choice
  var sallyAction = function(deceptive, goal) {
    if (deceptive) {
      // Deceptive: avoid goal food - uniform over actions that don't lead to goal
      return uniformDrift({a: 0, b: 1, width: 0.25});
    } else {
      // Honest: pick action whose outcome IS goal food
      return (goal === 'bagel') ? 'a' :
             (goal === 'cookie') ? 'b' :
             'c';
    }
  };
  
  // Observe Sally chooses 'b' twice
  var action1 = sallyAction(deceptive, goal);
  condition(action1 === 'b');
  
  var action2 = sallyAction(deceptive, goal);
  condition(action2 === 'b');
  
  return goal;
};

var ANSWER = Infer({method: 'MCMC', samples: 10000}, model);
var model = function() {
  var goalFood = uniformDraw(['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut']);
  var deceptive = flip(0.5);
  
  // Probability of choosing action 'b' given goal and deceptiveness
  var probChooseB = function() {
    if (!deceptive) {
      // If not deceptive, pick action whose outcome IS goal food
      if (goalFood === 'bagel') {
        return 0; // Would choose 'a', never 'b'
      } else if (goalFood === 'cookie') {
        return 1; // Would choose 'b'
      } else {
        return 0; // Would choose 'c', never 'b'
      }
    } else {
      // If deceptive, avoid goal food
      if (goalFood === 'bagel') {
        return 0.5; // Can choose 'b' or 'c', uniform
      } else if (goalFood === 'cookie') {
        return 0; // Cannot choose 'b' (avoids goal)
      } else {
        return 0.5; // Can choose 'a' or 'b', uniform
      }
    }
  };
  
  var prob = probChooseB();
  // Observe 'b' twice in a row
  factor(Math.log(prob) + Math.log(prob));
  
  return goalFood;
};

var ANSWER = Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, model);
var ANSWER = Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
  var goalFood = uniformDraw(['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut']);
  var deceptive = flip(0.5);
  
  var getProb = function(goal, action, isDeceptive) {
    if (!isDeceptive) {
      // Non-deceptive: picks the action that yields her goal food
      var goalAction = (goal === 'bagel' ? 'a' : goal === 'cookie' ? 'b' : 'c');
      return action === goalAction ? 1 : 1e-10;
    } else {
      // Deceptive: avoids the action for her goal, chooses uniformly from other two
      var avoidAction = (goal === 'bagel' ? 'a' : goal === 'cookie' ? 'b' : 'c');
      return action !== avoidAction ? 0.5 : 1e-10;
    }
  };
  
  // Condition on observing 'b' twice in a row
  factor(Math.log(getProb(goalFood, 'b', deceptive)));
  factor(Math.log(getProb(goalFood, 'b', deceptive)));
  
  return goalFood;
});
var model = function() {
  // Prior over goal food
  var goal = categorical({ps: [1/3, 1/3, 1/3], vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut']});
  
  // Prior over deceptive
  var deceptive = flip(0.5);
  
  // Vending machine outcomes given action
  var vendingMachine = function(action) {
    if (action === 'a') {
      return categorical({ps: [0.8, 0.1, 0.1], vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut']});
    } else if (action === 'b') {
      return categorical({ps: [0.1, 0.8, 0.1], vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut']});
    } else { // 'c'
      return categorical({ps: [0.1, 0.1, 0.8], vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut']});
    }
  };
  
  // Expected outcome of an action (most likely outcome)
  // For choosing action: if not deceptive, pick action whose outcome IS goal food
  // If deceptive, pick action whose outcome AVOIDS goal food
  
  // Probability that a given action is chosen
  // Non-deceptive: picks action whose outcome matches goal food
  // Deceptive: picks action whose outcome avoids goal food
  
  // We need to define what "picks an action whose outcome IS her goal food" means
  // and "avoids her goal food" means.
  // Let's interpret: non-deceptive picks the action most likely to give goal food
  // Deceptive picks an action that does NOT most likely give goal food
  
  // Best action for each goal:
  // goal=bagel -> best action is 'a'
  // goal=cookie -> best action is 'b'
  // goal=doughnut -> best action is 'c'
  
  var bestAction = function(g) {
    if (g === 'bagel') return 'a';
    if (g === 'cookie') return 'b';
    return 'c';
  };
  
  // Non-deceptive actions (actions whose outcome IS goal food = best action)
  // Deceptive actions: any action that is NOT the best action for goal
  // Among deceptive actions, uniform over the other two
  
  var actionLikelihood = function(action, g, dec) {
    var best = bestAction(g);
    if (!dec) {
      // picks the best action deterministically
      return action === best ? 1.0 : 0.0;
    } else {
      // avoids best action, uniform over the other two
      return action !== best ? 0.5 : 0.0;
    }
  };
  
  // Observe Sally chose 'b' twice
  factor(Math.log(actionLikelihood('b', goal, deceptive)));
  factor(Math.log(actionLikelihood('b', goal, deceptive)));
  
  return goal;
};

var ANSWER = Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, model);
var model = function() {
  // Prior over goal food
  var goal = categorical({vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut'], ps: [1/3, 1/3, 1/3]});
  
  // Prior over deceptive
  var deceptive = flip(0.5);
  
  // Vending machine outcomes given action
  var vendingMachine = function(action) {
    var ps = action === 'a' ? [0.8, 0.1, 0.1] :
             action === 'b' ? [0.1, 0.8, 0.1] :
                              [0.1, 0.1, 0.8];
    return categorical({vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut'], ps: ps});
  };
  
  // Sally picks action based on goal and deceptiveness
  // If not deceptive: picks action whose outcome IS her goal food (most likely)
  // If deceptive: avoids her goal food
  var chooseAction = function() {
    if (!deceptive) {
      // Pick the action most likely to give goal food
      // 'a' -> bagel, 'b' -> cookie, 'c' -> doughnut
      return goal === 'bagel' ? 'a' :
             goal === 'cookie' ? 'b' : 'c';
    } else {
      // Deceptive: avoid goal food, pick uniformly among non-goal actions
      var nonGoalActions = goal === 'bagel' ? ['b', 'c'] :
                           goal === 'cookie' ? ['a', 'c'] :
                                              ['a', 'b'];
      return nonGoalActions[randomInteger(2)];
    }
  };
  
  // Observe Sally choosing 'b' twice
  var action1 = chooseAction();
  condition(action1 === 'b');
  
  var action2 = chooseAction();
  condition(action2 === 'b');
  
  return goal;
};

var ANSWER = Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, model);
var model = function() {
  // Prior over Sally's true goal food
  var goal = uniformDraw(['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut']);

  // Prior over whether Sally is deceptive
  var deceptive = flip(0.5);

  // Action-selection policy
  var pickAction = function() {
    if (!deceptive) {
      // Non-deceptive: choose the action whose primary outcome IS the goal food
      if (goal === 'bagel')   return 'a';
      if (goal === 'cookie')  return 'b';
      return 'c'; // doughnut
    } else {
      // Deceptive: choose uniformly among actions that do NOT primarily give the goal food
      if (goal === 'bagel')   return uniformDraw(['b', 'c']);
      if (goal === 'cookie')  return uniformDraw(['a', 'c']);
      return uniformDraw(['a', 'b']); // doughnut
    }
  };

  // Observe 'b' twice (independent choices each time)
  var action1 = pickAction();
  condition(action1 === 'b');

  var action2 = pickAction();
  condition(action2 === 'b');

  return goal;
};

var ANSWER = Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, model);
var model = function() {
  // Prior over goal food
  var goal = categorical({vs: ['bagel', 'cookie', 'doughnut'],
                          ps: [1/3, 1/3, 1/3]});

  // Prior over deceptive vs honest
  var deceptive = flip(0.5);

  // Map each goal food to its "aligned" action and its "avoiding" actions
  var alignedAction = {bagel: 'a', cookie: 'b', doughnut: 'c'};
  var avoidingActions = {bagel: ['b', 'c'], cookie: ['a', 'c'], doughnut: ['a', 'b']};

  // Given goal and deceptive flag, return the probability of each action
  var actionProb = function(action) {
    if (!deceptive) {
      // Honest: deterministically picks the action aligned with goal
      return action === alignedAction[goal] ? 1.0 : 0.0;
    } else {
      // Deceptive: picks uniformly among actions that avoid goal food
      var avoiding = avoidingActions[goal];
      return _.includes(avoiding, action) ? (1.0 / avoiding.length) : 0.0;
    }
  };

  // Observe Sally choosing 'b' twice
  observe(Categorical({vs: ['a', 'b', 'c'], ps: [actionProb('a'), actionProb('b'), actionProb('c')]}), 'b');
  observe(Categorical({vs: ['a', 'b', 'c'], ps: [actionProb('a'), actionProb('b'), actionProb('c')]}), 'b');

  return goal;
};

var ANSWER = Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, model);
groundtruth output

doughnut0.1977
bagel0.1977
raw JSON

  "__kind": "distribution",
  "probs": [
    0.19767441860465113,
    0.6046511627906979,
    0.19767441860465113
  ],
  "support": [
    "doughnut",
    "cookie",
    "bagel"
  ]
}
probmods2-social-cognition/ex2.1exercisesrecord(stay, switch)8 runsTV=0.00TV=0
prompt
Monty Hall variant. Alice picks a door uniformly from {1, 2, 3} (one hides a prize, the others are empty). Monty opens a different door, and we condition on his door being neither Alice's nor the prize. In this variant, Monty picks a door uniformly at random from {1, 2, 3}, including possibly Alice's or the prize door (we filter those via the outer condition). Helpers (include them in your program): 
var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {
  return reduce(function(badItem, remainingL) {
    return remove(badItem, remainingL);
  }, l, badItems);
};
var doors = [1, 2, 3];
Build montyRandom(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) returning a Distribution over Monty's door under this variant. Build model(switches) that draws aliceDoor and prizeDoor uniformly, samples montyDoor from montyRandom, conditions on montyDoor != prizeDoor && montyDoor != aliceDoor, and returns aliceDoor == prizeDoor (whether Alice wins) under her strategy (switches true means switch to the remaining unopened door, false means stay). End your program with an object literal: stay: P(win) when Alice doesn't switch switch: P(win) when Alice switches Each value should be Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(<flag>); }).
groundtruth code
var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {
  return reduce(function(badItem, remainingL) {
    return remove(badItem, remainingL)
  }, l, badItems);
};

var doors = [1, 2, 3];

var montyRandom = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    return categorical({vs: doors});
  });
};

var model = function(switches) {
  var aliceDoor = categorical({vs: doors});
  var prizeDoor = categorical({vs: doors});
  var montyDoorDist = montyRandom(aliceDoor, prizeDoor);
  var montyDoor = sample(montyDoorDist);
  condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor);
  condition(montyDoor != aliceDoor);
  var aliceDoor = switches ? removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor, montyDoor])[0] : aliceDoor;
  return aliceDoor == prizeDoor;
};
var ANSWER = (({
  stay: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(false); }),
  switch: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(true); })
}));
generated code
haiku-45-noprimer-v3 failhaiku-45-primer-v3 TV<1haiku-45-think-noprimer-v3 TV=0haiku-45-think-primer-v3 TV<.5sonnet-46-noprimer-v3 TV<.5sonnet-46-primer-v3 TV=0sonnet-46-think-noprimer-v3 TV<.5sonnet-46-think-primer-v3 TV<.5
Error: Line 52: Did you mean var finalDoor = ?
var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {
  return reduce(function(badItem, remainingL) {
    return remove(badItem, remainingL);
  }, l, badItems);
};
var doors = [1, 2, 3];

var montyRandom = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var montyDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
    return montyDoor;
  });
};

var model = function(switches) {
  var aliceDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var prizeDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var montyDoor = sample(montyRandom(aliceDoor, prizeDoor));
  
  condition(montyDoor !== prizeDoor && montyDoor !== aliceDoor);
  
  var finalDoor;
  if (switches) {
    // Switch to the remaining unopened door
    var remainingDoors = removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor, montyDoor]);
    finalDoor = remainingDoors[0];
  } else {
    // Stay with Alice's original door
    finalDoor = aliceDoor;
  }
  
  return finalDoor === prizeDoor;
};

var ANSWER = {
  stay: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(false); }),
  switch: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(true); })
};

    return remove(badItem, remainingL);
  }, l, badItems);
};

  });
};

  var aliceDoor = uniformDraw(doors);

  var montyDoor = sample(montyRandom(aliceDoor, prizeDoor));
  
  condition(montyDoor !== prizeDoor && montyDoor !== aliceDoor);

var montyRandom = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {
  return Categorical({vs: doors, ps: [1/3, 1/3, 1/3]});
};

var model = function(switches) {
  var aliceDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var prizeDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var montyDoor = sample(montyRandom(aliceDoor, prizeDoor));
  
  condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor && montyDoor != aliceDoor);
  
  if (switches) {
    var remainingDoors = removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor, montyDoor]);
    var remainingDoor = remainingDoors[0];
    return remainingDoor == prizeDoor;
  } else {
    return aliceDoor == prizeDoor;
  }
};

var ANSWER = {
  stay: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(false); }),
  switch: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(true); })
};

probmods2-social-cognition/ex2.2exercisesrecord(stay, switch)8 runsTV=0.00TV=0
prompt
Monty Hall variant. Alice picks a door uniformly from {1, 2, 3} (one hides a prize, the others are empty). Monty opens a different door, and we condition on his door being neither Alice's nor the prize. In this variant, Monty deliberately picks a door that is neither Alice's nor the prize (this is the original Monty Hall setup). Inside montyAvoidBoth, sample Monty's door uniformly and condition that it is not Alice's and not the prize. Helpers (include them in your program): 
var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {
  return reduce(function(badItem, remainingL) {
    return remove(badItem, remainingL);
  }, l, badItems);
};
var doors = [1, 2, 3];
Build montyAvoidBoth(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) returning a Distribution over Monty's door under this variant. Build model(switches) that draws aliceDoor and prizeDoor uniformly, samples montyDoor from montyAvoidBoth, conditions on montyDoor != prizeDoor && montyDoor != aliceDoor, and returns aliceDoor == prizeDoor (whether Alice wins) under her strategy (switches true means switch to the remaining unopened door, false means stay). End your program with an object literal: stay: P(win) when Alice doesn't switch switch: P(win) when Alice switches Each value should be Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(<flag>); }).
groundtruth code
var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {
  return reduce(function(badItem, remainingL) {
    return remove(badItem, remainingL)
  }, l, badItems);
};

var doors = [1, 2, 3];

var montyAvoidBoth = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var montyDoor = categorical({vs: doors});
    condition(montyDoor != aliceDoor);
    condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor);
    return montyDoor;
  });
};

var model = function(switches) {
  var aliceDoor = categorical({vs: doors});
  var prizeDoor = categorical({vs: doors});
  var montyDoorDist = montyAvoidBoth(aliceDoor, prizeDoor);
  var montyDoor = sample(montyDoorDist);
  condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor);
  condition(montyDoor != aliceDoor);
  var aliceDoor = switches ? removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor, montyDoor])[0] : aliceDoor;
  return aliceDoor == prizeDoor;
};
var ANSWER = (({
  stay: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(false); }),
  switch: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(true); })
}));

    finalDoor = remainingDoors[0];
  } else {

    finalDoor = aliceDoor;
  }
  
  return finalDoor === prizeDoor;
};

var model = function(switches) {

var ANSWER = {

var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {
  return reduce(function(badItem, remainingL) {
    return remove(badItem, remainingL);
  }, l, badItems);
};
var doors = [1, 2, 3];

var montyAvoidBoth = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var montyDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
    condition(montyDoor != aliceDoor && montyDoor != prizeDoor);
    return montyDoor;
  });
};

var model = function(switches) {
  var aliceDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var prizeDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var montyDoor = sample(montyAvoidBoth(aliceDoor, prizeDoor));
  
  // The remaining unopened door (not Alice's, not Monty's)
  var remainingDoors = removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor, montyDoor]);
  var switchDoor = remainingDoors[0];
  
  var finalDoor = switches ? switchDoor : aliceDoor;
  return finalDoor == prizeDoor;
};

var ANSWER = {
  stay: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(false); }),
  switch: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(true); })
};

var doors = [1, 2, 3];

var montyAvoidBoth = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var montyDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
    condition(montyDoor != aliceDoor && montyDoor != prizeDoor);
    return montyDoor;
  });
};

var model = function(switches) {
  var aliceDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var prizeDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var montyDoor = sample(montyAvoidBoth(aliceDoor, prizeDoor));
  condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor && montyDoor != aliceDoor);
  var finalDoor = switches
    ? uniformDraw(removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor, montyDoor]))
    : aliceDoor;
  return finalDoor == prizeDoor;
};

var ANSWER = {
  stay: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(false); }),
  switch: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(true); })
};

prompt
Monty Hall variant. Alice picks a door uniformly from {1, 2, 3} (one hides a prize, the others are empty). Monty opens a different door, and we condition on his door being neither Alice's nor the prize. In this variant, Monty picks a door uniformly at random but only avoids Alice's door (he may inadvertently reveal the prize). Inside montyAvoidAlice, sample Monty's door uniformly and condition only that it is not Alice's. Helpers (include them in your program): 
var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {
  return reduce(function(badItem, remainingL) {
    return remove(badItem, remainingL);
  }, l, badItems);
};
var doors = [1, 2, 3];
Build montyAvoidAlice(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) returning a Distribution over Monty's door under this variant. Build model(switches) that draws aliceDoor and prizeDoor uniformly, samples montyDoor from montyAvoidAlice, conditions on montyDoor != prizeDoor && montyDoor != aliceDoor, and returns aliceDoor == prizeDoor (whether Alice wins) under her strategy (switches true means switch to the remaining unopened door, false means stay). End your program with an object literal: stay: P(win) when Alice doesn't switch switch: P(win) when Alice switches Each value should be Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(<flag>); }).
groundtruth code
var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {
  return reduce(function(badItem, remainingL) {
    return remove(badItem, remainingL)
  }, l, badItems);
};

var doors = [1, 2, 3];

var montyAvoidAlice = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var montyDoor = categorical({vs: doors});
    condition(montyDoor != aliceDoor);
    return montyDoor;
  });
};

var model = function(switches) {
  var aliceDoor = categorical({vs: doors});
  var prizeDoor = categorical({vs: doors});
  var montyDoorDist = montyAvoidAlice(aliceDoor, prizeDoor);
  var montyDoor = sample(montyDoorDist);
  condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor);
  condition(montyDoor != aliceDoor);
  var aliceDoor = switches ? removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor, montyDoor])[0] : aliceDoor;
  return aliceDoor == prizeDoor;
};
var ANSWER = (({
  stay: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(false); }),
  switch: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(true); })
}));

Error: Line 52: Did you mean var finalDoor = ?
var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {
  return reduce(function(badItem, remainingL) {
    return remove(badItem, remainingL);
  }, l, badItems);
};
var doors = [1, 2, 3];

var montyAvoidAlice = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var montyDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
    condition(montyDoor !== aliceDoor);
    return montyDoor;
  });
};

var model = function(switches) {
  var aliceDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var prizeDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var montyDoor = sample(montyAvoidAlice(aliceDoor, prizeDoor));
  
  condition(montyDoor !== prizeDoor && montyDoor !== aliceDoor);
  
  var finalDoor;
  if (switches) {
    var remainingDoors = removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor, montyDoor]);
    finalDoor = remainingDoors[0];
  } else {
    finalDoor = aliceDoor;
  }
  
  return finalDoor === prizeDoor;
};

var ANSWER = {
  stay: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(false); }),
  switch: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(true); })
};

var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {

var doors = [1, 2, 3];

var montyAvoidAlice = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var validDoors = remove(aliceDoor, doors);
    var montyDoor = sample(Categorical({vs: validDoors, ps: [0.5, 0.5]}));
    return montyDoor;
  });
};

var model = function(switches) {
  var aliceDoor = sample(Categorical({vs: doors, ps: [1/3, 1/3, 1/3]}));
  var prizeDoor = sample(Categorical({vs: doors, ps: [1/3, 1/3, 1/3]}));
  var montyDoor = sample(montyAvoidAlice(aliceDoor, prizeDoor));
  
  condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor && montyDoor != aliceDoor);

    var remainingDoors = removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor, montyDoor]);
    finalDoor = remainingDoors[0];
  } else {
    finalDoor = aliceDoor;
  }
  
  return finalDoor == prizeDoor;
};

var ANSWER = {
  stay: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(false); }),
  switch: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(true); })
};

var montyAvoidAlice = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {

    var montyDoor = uniformDraw(removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor]));

  });

var model = function(switches) {
  var aliceDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var prizeDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var montyDoor = sample(montyAvoidAlice(aliceDoor, prizeDoor));
  condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor && montyDoor != aliceDoor);
  var finalDoor = switches

var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {
  return reduce(function(badItem, remainingL) {
    return remove(badItem, remainingL);
  }, l, badItems);
};
var doors = [1, 2, 3];

var montyAvoidAlice = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    // Monty picks uniformly from doors that are not Alice's door
    var montyDoor = uniformDraw(removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor]));
    return montyDoor;
  });
};

var model = function(switches) {
  var aliceDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var prizeDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var montyDoor = sample(montyAvoidAlice(aliceDoor, prizeDoor));
  // Condition that Monty didn't accidentally reveal the prize and didn't open Alice's door
  condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor && montyDoor != aliceDoor);
  // Determine Alice's final door based on strategy
  var finalDoor = switches
    ? uniformDraw(removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor, montyDoor]))
    : aliceDoor;
  return finalDoor == prizeDoor;
};

var ANSWER = {
  stay:   Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(false); }),
  switch: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(true); })
};
var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {
  return reduce(function(badItem, remainingL) {
    return remove(badItem, remainingL);
  }, l, badItems);
};

var doors = [1, 2, 3];

var montyAvoidAlice = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var availableDoors = removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor]);
    var montyDoor = uniformDraw(availableDoors);
    return montyDoor;
  });
};

var model = function(switches) {
  var aliceDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var prizeDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var montyDoor = sample(montyAvoidAlice(aliceDoor, prizeDoor));
  condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor && montyDoor != aliceDoor);
  var remainingDoor = removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor, montyDoor])[0];
  var finalDoor = switches ? remainingDoor : aliceDoor;
  return finalDoor == prizeDoor;
};

var ANSWER = {
  stay: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(false); }),
  switch: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(true); })
};

prompt
Monty Hall variant. Alice picks a door uniformly from {1, 2, 3} (one hides a prize, the others are empty). Monty opens a different door, and we condition on his door being neither Alice's nor the prize. In this variant, Monty picks a door uniformly at random but only avoids the prize door (he may inadvertently pick Alice's). Inside montyAvoidPrize, sample Monty's door uniformly and condition only that it is not the prize. Helpers (include them in your program): 
var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {
  return reduce(function(badItem, remainingL) {
    return remove(badItem, remainingL);
  }, l, badItems);
};
var doors = [1, 2, 3];
Build montyAvoidPrize(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) returning a Distribution over Monty's door under this variant. Build model(switches) that draws aliceDoor and prizeDoor uniformly, samples montyDoor from montyAvoidPrize, conditions on montyDoor != prizeDoor && montyDoor != aliceDoor, and returns aliceDoor == prizeDoor (whether Alice wins) under her strategy (switches true means switch to the remaining unopened door, false means stay). End your program with an object literal: stay: P(win) when Alice doesn't switch switch: P(win) when Alice switches Each value should be Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(<flag>); }).
groundtruth code
var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {
  return reduce(function(badItem, remainingL) {
    return remove(badItem, remainingL)
  }, l, badItems);
};

var doors = [1, 2, 3];

var montyAvoidPrize = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var montyDoor = categorical({vs: doors});
    condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor);
    return montyDoor;
  });
};

var model = function(switches) {
  var aliceDoor = categorical({vs: doors});
  var prizeDoor = categorical({vs: doors});
  var montyDoorDist = montyAvoidPrize(aliceDoor, prizeDoor);
  var montyDoor = sample(montyDoorDist);
  condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor);
  condition(montyDoor != aliceDoor);
  var aliceDoor = switches ? removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor, montyDoor])[0] : aliceDoor;
  return aliceDoor == prizeDoor;
};
var ANSWER = (({
  stay: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(false); }),
  switch: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(true); })
}));

var montyAvoidPrize = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {

    var montyDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
    condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor);
    return montyDoor;
  });

var model = function(switches) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var aliceDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
    var prizeDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
    var montyDoor = sample(montyAvoidPrize(aliceDoor, prizeDoor));
    
    condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor && montyDoor != aliceDoor);
    
    var finalDoor;
    if (switches) {
      // Alice switches to the remaining unopened door
      var remainingDoors = removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor, montyDoor]);
      finalDoor = remainingDoors[0];
    } else {
      // Alice stays with her original door
      finalDoor = aliceDoor;
    }
    
    return finalDoor == prizeDoor;
  });
};

var ANSWER = {
  stay: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(false); }),
  switch: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(true); })
};
var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {
  return reduce(function(badItem, remainingL) {
    return remove(badItem, remainingL);
  }, l, badItems);
};
var doors = [1, 2, 3];

var montyAvoidPrize = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var montyDoor = sample(Categorical({vs: [1, 2, 3], ps: [1/3, 1/3, 1/3]}));
    condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor);
    return montyDoor;
  });
};

var model = function(switches) {
  var aliceDoor = sample(Categorical({vs: [1, 2, 3], ps: [1/3, 1/3, 1/3]}));
  var prizeDoor = sample(Categorical({vs: [1, 2, 3], ps: [1/3, 1/3, 1/3]}));
  var montyDoor = sample(montyAvoidPrize(aliceDoor, prizeDoor));
  
  // Condition on montyDoor being neither Alice's nor the prize
  condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor && montyDoor != aliceDoor);
  
  // Determine the final door
  var finalDoor = switches ? 
    // If switching, pick the remaining door (not Alice's, not Monty's)
    (_.filter(function(d) { return d != aliceDoor && d != montyDoor; }, doors)[0]) :
    // If staying, keep Alice's door
    aliceDoor;
  
  return finalDoor == prizeDoor;
};

var ANSWER = {
  stay: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(false); }),
  switch: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(true); })
};
var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {
  return reduce(function(badItem, remainingL) {
    return remove(badItem, remainingL);
  }, l, badItems);
};
var doors = [1, 2, 3];

var montyAvoidPrize = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var montyDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
    condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor);
    return montyDoor;
  });
};

var model = function(switches) {
  var aliceDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var prizeDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var montyDoor = sample(montyAvoidPrize(aliceDoor, prizeDoor));
  condition(montyDoor != aliceDoor);
  
  var finalDoor = switches
    ? uniformDraw(removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor, montyDoor]))
    : aliceDoor;
  
  return finalDoor == prizeDoor;
};

var ANSWER = {
  stay: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(false); }),
  switch: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(true); })
};

var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {
  return reduce(function(badItem, remainingL) {
    return remove(badItem, remainingL);
  }, l, badItems);
};
var doors = [1, 2, 3];

var montyAvoidPrize = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var montyDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
    condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor);
    return montyDoor;
  });
};

var model = function(switches) {
  var aliceDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var prizeDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var montyDoor = sample(montyAvoidPrize(aliceDoor, prizeDoor));
  condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor && montyDoor != aliceDoor);

  var finalDoor = switches
    ? uniformDraw(removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor, montyDoor]))
    : aliceDoor;

  return finalDoor == prizeDoor;
};

var ANSWER = {
  stay:   Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(false); }),
  switch: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(true);  })
};
var removeBadItems = function(l, badItems) {
  return reduce(function(badItem, remainingL) {
    return remove(badItem, remainingL);
  }, l, badItems);
};
var doors = [1, 2, 3];

var montyAvoidPrize = function(aliceDoor, prizeDoor) {
  return Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() {
    var montyDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
    condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor);
    return montyDoor;
  });
};

var model = function(switches) {
  var aliceDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var prizeDoor = uniformDraw(doors);
  var montyDoor = sample(montyAvoidPrize(aliceDoor, prizeDoor));
  condition(montyDoor != prizeDoor && montyDoor != aliceDoor);
  var remainingDoor = removeBadItems(doors, [aliceDoor, montyDoor])[0];
  var finalDoor = switches ? remainingDoor : aliceDoor;
  return finalDoor == prizeDoor;
};

var ANSWER = {
  stay:   Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(false); }),
  switch: Infer({method: 'enumerate'}, function() { return model(true); })
};
groundtruth output
{
  "stay": {
    "__kind": "distribution",
    "probs": [
      0.5,
      0.5
    ],
    "support": [
      true,
      false
    ]
  },
  "switch": {
    "__kind": "distribution",
    "probs": [
      0.5,
      0.5
    ],
    "support": [
      false,
      true
    ]
  }
}
